chapters.en: But the children of Israel walked upon dry in the midst of the sea; and the waters a wall unto them on their right hand, and on their left.

chapters.zh: 当日，耶和华这样拯救以色列人脱离埃及人的手，以色列人看见埃及人的死尸都在海边了。
chapters.en: Thus the LORD saved Israel that day out of the hand of the Egyptians; and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the sea shore.

chapters.zh: 以色列人看见耶和华向埃及人所行的大事，就敬畏耶和华，又信服他和他的仆人摩西。
chapters.en: And Israel saw that great work which the LORD did upon the Egyptians: and the people feared the LORD, and believed the LORD, and his servant Moses.

chapters.zh: 那时，摩西和以色列人向耶和华唱歌说：我要向耶和华歌唱，因他大大战胜，将马和骑马的投在海中。
chapters.en: Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song unto the LORD, and spake, saying, I will sing unto the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously: the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是我的力量，我的诗歌，也成了我的拯救。这是我的神，我要赞美他，是我父亲的神，我要尊崇他。
chapters.en: The LORD my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he my God, and I will prepare him an habitation; my father's God, and I will exalt him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华是战士；他的名是耶和华。
chapters.en: The LORD a man of war: the LORD his name.

chapters.zh: 法老的车辆、军兵，耶和华已抛在海中；他特选的军长都沉于红海。
chapters.en: Pharaoh's chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea: his chosen captains also are drowned in the Red sea.

chapters.zh: 深水淹没他们；他们如同石头坠到深处。
chapters.en: The depths have covered them: they sank into the bottom as a stone.

chapters.zh: 耶和华阿，你的右手施展能力，显出荣耀；耶和华阿，你的右手摔碎仇敌。
chapters.en: Thy right hand, O LORD, is become glorious in power: thy right hand, O LORD, hath dashed in pieces the enemy.

chapters.zh: 你大发威严，推翻那些起来攻击你的；你发出烈怒如火，烧灭他们像烧碎一样。
chapters.en: And in the greatness of thine excellency thou hast overthrown them that rose up against thee: thou sentest forth thy wrath, consumed them as stubble.

chapters.zh: 你发鼻中的气，水便聚起成堆，大水直立如垒，海中的深水凝结。
chapters.en: And with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were gathered together, the floods stood upright as an heap, the depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.

chapters.zh: 仇敌说：我要追赶，我要追上；我要分掳物，我要在他们身上称我的心愿。我要拔出刀来，亲手杀灭他们。
chapters.en: The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my lust shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.

chapters.zh: 你叫风一吹，海就把他们淹没；他们如铅沉在大水之中。
chapters.en: Thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them: they sank as lead in the mighty waters.

chapters.zh: 耶和华阿，众神之中，谁能像你？谁能像你 ─ 至圣至荣，可颂可畏，施行奇事？
chapters.en: Who like unto thee, O LORD, among the gods? who like thee, glorious in holiness, fearful praises, doing wonders?

chapters.zh: 你伸出右手，地便吞灭他们。
chapters.en: Thou stretchedst out thy right hand, the earth swallowed them.

chapters.zh: 你凭慈爱领了你所赎的百姓；你凭能力引他们到了你的圣所。
chapters.en: Thou in thy mercy hast led forth the people thou hast redeemed: thou hast guided in thy strength unto thy holy habitation.

chapters.zh: 外邦人听见就发颤；疼痛抓住非利士的居民。
chapters.en: The people shall hear, be afraid: sorrow shall take hold on the inhabitants of Palestina.

chapters.zh: 那时，以东的族长惊惶，摩押的英雄被战兢抓住，迦南的居民心都消化了。
chapters.en: Then the dukes of Edom shall be amazed; the mighty men of Moab, trembling shall take hold upon them; all the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away.

chapters.zh: 惊骇恐惧临到他们。耶和华阿，因你膀臂的大能，他们如石头寂然不动，等候你的百姓过去，等候你所赎的百姓过去。
chapters.en: Fear and dread shall fall upon them; by the greatness of thine arm they shall be still as a stone; till thy people pass over, O LORD, till the people pass over, thou hast purchased.

chapters.zh: 你要将他们领进去，栽于你产业的山上 ─ 耶和华阿，就是你为自己所造的住处；主阿，就是你手所建立的圣所。
chapters.en: Thou shalt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain of thine inheritance, the place, O LORD, thou hast made for thee to dwell in, the Sanctuary, O Lord, thy hands have established.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必作王，直到永永远远！
chapters.en: The LORD shall reign for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: 法老的马匹、车辆，和马兵下到海中，耶和华使海水回流，淹没他们；惟有以色列人在海中走干地。
chapters.en: For the horse of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the LORD brought again the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry in the midst of the sea.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的姊姊，女先知米利暗，手里拿著鼓；众妇女也跟他出去拿鼓跳舞。
chapters.en: And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.

chapters.zh: 米利暗应声说：你们要歌颂耶和华，因他大大战胜，将马和骑马的投在海中。
chapters.en: And Miriam answered them, Sing ye to the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously; the horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

chapters.zh: 摩西领以色列人从红海往前行，到了书珥的旷野，在旷野走了三天，找不著水。
chapters.en: So Moses brought Israel from the Red sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.

chapters.zh: 到了玛拉，不能喝那里的水；因为水苦，所以那地名叫玛拉。
chapters.en: And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they bitter: therefore the name of it was called Marah.

chapters.zh: 百姓就向摩西发怨言，说：我们喝甚么呢？
chapters.en: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?

chapters.zh: 摩西呼求耶和华，耶和华指示他一棵树。他把树丢在水里，水就变甜了。耶和华在那里为他们定了律例、典章，在那里试验他们；
chapters.en: And he cried unto the LORD; and the LORD shewed him a tree, when he had cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet: there he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them,

chapters.zh: 又说：你若留意听耶和华 ─ 你神的话，又行我眼中看为正的事，留心听我的诫命，守我一切的律例，我就不将所加与埃及人的疾病加在你身上，因为我 ─ 耶和华是医治你的。
chapters.en: And said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the LORD thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee, which I have brought upon the Egyptians: for I the LORD that healeth thee.

chapters.zh: 他们到了以琳，在那里有十二股水泉，七十棵棕树；他们就在那里的水边安营。
chapters.en: And they came to Elim, where twelve wells of water, and threescore and ten palm trees: and they encamped there by the waters.

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众从以琳起行，在出埃及后第二个月十五日到了以琳和西乃中间、汛的旷野。
chapters.en: And they took their journey from Elim, and all the congregation of the children of Israel came unto the wilderness of Sin, which between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departing out of the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众在旷野向摩西、亚伦发怨言，
chapters.en: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness:

chapters.zh: 说：巴不得我们早死在埃及地、耶和华的手下；那时我们坐在肉锅旁边，吃得饱足。你们将我们领出来，到这旷野，是要叫这全会众都饿死阿！
chapters.en: And the children of Israel said unto them, Would to God we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the flesh pots, when we did eat bread to the full; for ye have brought us forth into this wilderness, to kill this whole assembly with hunger.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：我要将粮食从天降给你们。百姓可以出去，每天收每天的分，我好试验他们遵不遵我的法度。
chapters.en: Then said the LORD unto Moses, Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you; and the people shall go out and gather a certain rate every day, that I may prove them, whether they will walk in my law, or no.

chapters.zh: 到第六天，他们要把所收进来的预备好了，比每天所收的多一倍。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, that on the sixth day they shall prepare which they bring in; and it shall be twice as much as they gather daily.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦对以色列众人说：到了晚上，你们要知道是耶和华将你们从埃及地领出来的。
chapters.en: And Moses and Aaron said unto all the children of Israel, At even, then ye shall know that the LORD hath brought you out from the land of Egypt:

chapters.zh: 早晨，你们要看见耶和华的荣耀，因为耶和华听见你们向他所发的怨言了。我们算甚么，你们竟向我们发怨言呢？
chapters.en: And in the morning, then ye shall see the glory of the LORD; for that he heareth your murmurings against the LORD: and what we, that ye murmur against us?

chapters.zh: 摩西又说：耶和华晚上必给你们肉吃，早晨必给你们食物得饱；因为你们向耶和华发的怨言，他都听见了。我们算甚么，你们的怨言不是向我们发的，乃是向耶和华发的。
chapters.en: And Moses said, , when the LORD shall give you in the evening flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full; for that the LORD heareth your murmurings which ye murmur against him: and what we? your murmurings not against us, but against the LORD.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦说：你告诉以色列全会众说：你们就近耶和华面前，因为他已经听见你们的怨言了。
chapters.en: And Moses spake unto Aaron, Say unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, Come near before the LORD: for he hath heard your murmurings.

chapters.zh: 亚伦正对以色列全会众说话的时候，他们向旷野观看，不料，耶和华的荣光在云中显现。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as Aaron spake unto the whole congregation of the children of Israel, that they looked toward the wilderness, and, behold, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 我已经听见以色列人的怨言。你告诉他们说：到黄昏的时候，你们要吃肉，早晨必有食物得饱，你们就知道我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel: speak unto them, saying, At even ye shall eat flesh, and in the morning ye shall be filled with bread; and ye shall know that I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 到了晚上，有鹌鹑飞来，遮满了营；早晨在营四围的地上有露水。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that at even the quails came up, and covered the camp: and in the morning the dew lay round about the host.

chapters.zh: 露水上升之后，不料，野地面上有如白霜的小圆物。
chapters.en: And when the dew that lay was gone up, behold, upon the face of the wilderness a small round thing, small as the hoar frost on the ground.

chapters.zh: 以色列人看见，不知道是甚么，就彼此对问说：这是甚么呢？摩西对他们说：这就是耶和华给你们吃的食物。
chapters.en: And when the children of Israel saw , they said one to another, It manna: for they wist not what it . And Moses said unto them, This the bread which the LORD hath given you to eat.

chapters.zh: 耶和华所吩咐的是这样：你们要按著各人的饭量，为帐棚里的人，按著人数收起来，各拿一俄梅珥。
chapters.en: This the thing which the LORD hath commanded, Gather of it every man according to his eating, an omer for every man, the number of your persons; take ye every man for which in his tents.

chapters.zh: 以色列人就这样行；有多收的，有少收的。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel did so, and gathered, some more, some less.

chapters.zh: 及至用俄梅珥量一量，多收的也没有余，少收的也没有缺；各人按著自己的饭量收取。
chapters.en: And when they did mete with an omer, he that gathered much had nothing over, and he that gathered little had no lack; they gathered every man according to his eating.

chapters.zh: 摩西对他们说：所收的，不许甚么人留到早晨。
chapters.en: And Moses said, Let no man leave of it till the morning.

chapters.zh: 然而他们不听摩西的话，内中有留到早晨的，就生虫变臭了；摩西便向他们发怒。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto Moses; but some of them left of it until the morning, and it bred worms, and stank: and Moses was wroth with them.

chapters.zh: 他们每日早晨，按著各人的饭量收取，日头一发热，就消化了。
chapters.en: And they gathered it every morning, every man according to his eating: and when the sun waxed hot, it melted.

chapters.zh: 到第六天，他们收了双倍的食物，每人两俄梅珥。会众的官长来告诉摩西；
chapters.en: And it came to pass, on the sixth day they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for one : and all the rulers of the congregation came and told Moses.

chapters.zh: 摩西对他们说：耶和华这样说：明天是圣安息日，是向耶和华守的圣安息日。你们要烤的就烤了，要煮的就煮了，所剩下的都留到早晨。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, This which the LORD hath said, To morrow the rest of the holy sabbath unto the LORD: bake which ye will bake , and seethe that ye will seethe; and that which remaineth over lay up for you to be kept until the morning.

chapters.zh: 他们就照摩西的吩咐留到早晨，也不臭，里头也没有虫子。
chapters.en: And they laid it up till the morning, as Moses bade: and it did not stink, neither was there any worm therein.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：你们今天吃这个罢！因为今天是向耶和华守的安息日；你们在田野必找不著了。
chapters.en: And Moses said, Eat that to day; for to day a sabbath unto the LORD: to day ye shall not find it in the field.

chapters.zh: 六天可以收取，第七天乃是安息日，那一天必没有了。
chapters.en: Six days ye shall gather it; but on the seventh day, the sabbath, in it there shall be none.

chapters.zh: 第七天，百姓中有人出去收，甚么也找不著。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, there went out of the people on the seventh day for to gather, and they found none.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你们不肯守我的诫命和律法，要到几时呢？
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, How long refuse ye to keep my commandments and my laws?

chapters.zh: 你们看！耶和华既将安息日赐给你们，所以第六天他赐给你们两天的食物，第七天各人要住在自己的地方，不许甚么人出去。
chapters.en: See, for that the LORD hath given you the sabbath, therefore he giveth you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide ye every man in his place, let no man go out of his place on the seventh day.

chapters.zh: 于是百姓第七天安息了。
chapters.en: So the people rested on the seventh day.

chapters.zh: 这食物，以色列家叫吗哪；样子像芫荽子，颜色是白的，滋味如同搀蜜的薄饼。
chapters.en: And the house of Israel called the name thereof Manna: and it like coriander seed, white; and the taste of it like wafers with honey.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：耶和华所吩咐的是这样：要将一满俄梅珥俄梅珥就是伊法十分之一）吗哪留到世世代代，使后人可以看见我当日将你们领出埃及地，在旷野所给你们吃的食物。
chapters.en: And Moses said, This the thing which the LORD commandeth, Fill an omer of it to be kept for your generations; that they may see the bread wherewith I have fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you forth from the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦说：你拿一个罐子，盛一满俄梅珥吗哪，存在耶和华面前，要留到世世代代。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a pot, and put an omer full of manna therein, and lay it up before the LORD, to be kept for your generations.

chapters.zh: 耶和华怎么吩咐摩西，亚伦就怎么行，把吗哪放在法柜前存留。
chapters.en: As the LORD commanded Moses, so Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept.

chapters.zh: 以色列人吃吗哪共四十年，直到进了有人居住之地，就是迦南的境界。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel did eat manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they did eat manna, until they came unto the borders of the land of Canaan.

chapters.zh: 俄梅珥就是伊法十分之一。
chapters.en: Now an omer the tenth of an ephah.

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众都遵耶和华的吩咐，按著站口从汛的旷野往前行，在利非订安营。百姓没有水喝，
chapters.en: And all the congregation of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilderness of Sin, after their journeys, according to the commandment of the LORD, and pitched in Rephidim: and no water for the people to drink.

chapters.zh: 所以与摩西争闹，说：给我们水喝罢！摩西对他们说：你们为甚么与我争闹？为甚么试探耶和华呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore the people did chide with Moses, and said, Give us water that we may drink. And Moses said unto them, Why chide ye with me? wherefore do ye tempt the LORD?

chapters.zh: 百姓在那里甚渴，要喝水，就向摩西发怨言，说：你为甚么将我们从埃及领出来，使我们和我们的儿女并牲畜都渴死呢？
chapters.en: And the people thirsted there for water; and the people murmured against Moses, and said, Wherefore this thou hast brought us up out of Egypt, to kill us and our children and our cattle with thirst?

chapters.zh: 摩西就呼求耶和华说：我向这百姓怎样行呢？他们几乎要拿石头打死我。
chapters.en: And Moses cried unto the LORD, saying, What shall I do unto this people? they be almost ready to stone me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你手里拿著你先前击打河水的杖，带领以色列的几个长老，从百姓面前走过去。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Go on before the people, and take with thee of the elders of Israel; and thy rod, wherewith thou smotest the river, take in thine hand, and go.

chapters.zh: 我必在何烈的磐石那里，站在你面前。你要击打磐石，从磐石里必有水流出来，使百姓可以喝。摩西就在以色列的长老眼前这样行了。
chapters.en: Behold, I will stand before thee there upon the rock in Horeb; and thou shalt smite the rock, and there shall come water out of it, that the people may drink. And Moses did so in the sight of the elders of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他给那地方起名叫玛撒（就是试探的意思），又叫米利巴（就是争闹的意思）；因以色列人争闹，又因他们试探耶和华，说：耶和华是在我们中间不是？
chapters.en: And he called the name of the place Massah, and Meribah, because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the LORD, saying, Is the LORD among us, or not?

chapters.zh: 那时，亚玛力人来在利非订，和以色列人争战。
chapters.en: Then came Amalek, and fought with Israel in Rephidim.

chapters.zh: 摩西对约书亚说：你为我们选出人来，出去和亚玛力人争战。明天我手里要拿著神的杖，站在山顶上。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Joshua, Choose us out men, and go out, fight with Amalek: to morrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in mine hand.

chapters.zh: 于是约书亚照著摩西对他所说的话行，和亚玛力人争战。摩西、亚伦，与户珥都上了山顶。
chapters.en: So Joshua did as Moses had said to him, and fought with Amalek: and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

chapters.zh: 摩西何时举手，以色列人就得胜，何时垂手，亚玛力人就得胜。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when Moses held up his hand, that Israel prevailed: and when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.

chapters.zh: 但摩西的手发沉，他们就搬石头来，放在他以下，他就坐在上面。亚伦与户珥扶著他的手，一个在这边，一个在那边，他的手就稳住，直到日落的时候。
chapters.en: But Moses' hands heavy; and they took a stone, and put under him, and he sat thereon; and Aaron and Hur stayed up his hands, the one on the one side, and the other on the other side; and his hands were steady until the going down of the sun.

chapters.zh: 约书亚用刀杀了亚玛力王和他的百姓。
chapters.en: And Joshua discomfited Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：我要将亚玛力的名号从天下全然涂抹了；你要将这话写在书上作纪念，又念给约书亚听。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Write this a memorial in a book, and rehearse in the ears of Joshua: for I will utterly put out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven.

chapters.zh: 摩西筑了一座坛，起名叫耶和华尼西（就是耶和华是我旌旗的意思），
chapters.en: And Moses built an altar, and called the name of it Jehovahnissi:

chapters.zh: 又说：耶和华已经起了誓，必世世代代和亚玛力人争战。
chapters.en: For he said, Because the LORD hath sworn the LORD war with Amalek from generation to generation.

chapters.zh: 摩西的岳父，米甸祭司叶忒罗，听见神为摩西和神的百姓以色列所行的一切事，就是耶和华将以色列从埃及领出来的事，
chapters.en: When Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father in law, heard of all that God had done for Moses, and for Israel his people, that the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt;

chapters.zh: 便带著摩西的妻子西坡拉，就是摩西从前打发回去的，
chapters.en: Then Jethro, Moses' father in law, took Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her back,

chapters.zh: 又带著西坡拉的两个儿子，一个名叫革舜，因为摩西说：我在外邦作了寄居的；
chapters.en: And her two sons; of which the name of the one Gershom; for he said, I have been an alien in a strange land:

chapters.zh: 一个名叫以利以谢因为他说：我父亲的神帮助了我，救我脱离法老的刀。
chapters.en: And the name of the other Eliezer; for the God of my father, mine help, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh:

chapters.zh: 摩西的岳父叶忒罗带著摩西的妻子和两个儿子来到神的山，就是摩西在旷野安营的地方。
chapters.en: And Jethro, Moses' father in law, came with his sons and his wife unto Moses into the wilderness, where he encamped at the mount of God:

chapters.zh: 他对摩西说：我是你岳父叶忒罗，带著你的妻子和两个儿子来到你这里。
chapters.en: And he said unto Moses, I thy father in law Jethro am come unto thee, and thy wife, and her two sons with her.

chapters.zh: 摩西迎接他的岳父，向他下拜，与他亲嘴，彼此问安，都进了帐棚。
chapters.en: And Moses went out to meet his father in law, and did obeisance, and kissed him; and they asked each other of welfare; and they came into the tent.

chapters.zh: 摩西将耶和华为以色列的缘故向法老和埃及人所行的一切事，以及路上所遭遇的一切艰难，并耶和华怎样搭救他们，都述说与他岳父听。
chapters.en: And Moses told his father in law all that the LORD had done unto Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake, all the travail that had come upon them by the way, and the LORD delivered them.

chapters.zh: 叶忒罗因耶和华待以色列的一切好处，就是拯救他们脱离埃及人的手，便甚欢喜。
chapters.en: And Jethro rejoiced for all the goodness which the LORD had done to Israel, whom he had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.

chapters.zh: 叶忒罗说：耶和华是应当称颂的；他救了你们脱离埃及人和法老的手，将这百姓从埃及人的手下救出来。
chapters.en: And Jethro said, Blessed the LORD, who hath delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of Pharaoh, who hath delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.

chapters.zh: 我现今在埃及人向这百姓发狂傲的事上得知，耶和华比万神都大。
chapters.en: Now I know that the LORD greater than all gods: for in the thing wherein they dealt proudly above them.

chapters.zh: 摩西的岳父叶忒罗把燔祭和平安祭献给神。亚伦和以色列的众长老都来了，与摩西的岳父在神面前吃饭。
chapters.en: And Jethro, Moses' father in law, took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God: and Aaron came, and all the elders of Israel, to eat bread with Moses' father in law before God.

chapters.zh: 第二天，摩西坐著审判百姓，百姓从早到晚都站在摩西的左右。
chapters.en: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses sat to judge the people: and the people stood by Moses from the morning unto the evening.

chapters.zh: 摩西的岳父看见他向百姓所做的一切事，就说：你向百姓做的是甚么事呢？你为甚么独自坐著，众百姓从早到晚都站在你的左右呢？
chapters.en: And when Moses' father in law saw all that he did to the people, he said, What this thing that thou doest to the people? why sittest thou thyself alone, and all the people stand by thee from morning unto even?

chapters.zh: 摩西对岳父说：这是因百姓到我这里来求问神。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto his father in law, Because the people come unto me to enquire of God:

chapters.zh: 他们有事的时候就到我这里来，我便在两造之间施行审判；我又叫他们知道神的律例和法度。
chapters.en: When they have a matter, they come unto me; and I judge between one and another, and I do make know the statutes of God, and his laws.

chapters.zh: 摩西的岳父说：你这做的不好。
chapters.en: And Moses' father in law said unto him, The thing that thou doest not good.

chapters.zh: 你和这些百姓必都疲惫；因为这事太重，你独自一人办理不了。
chapters.en: Thou wilt surely wear away, both thou, and this people that with thee: for this thing too heavy for thee; thou art not able to perform it thyself alone.

chapters.zh: 现在你要听我的话。我为你出个主意，愿神与你同在。你要替百姓到神面前，将案件奏告神；
chapters.en: Hearken now unto my voice, I will give thee counsel, and God shall be with thee: Be thou for the people to God-ward, that thou mayest bring the causes unto God:

chapters.zh: 又要将律例和法度教训他们，指示他们当行的道，当做的事；
chapters.en: And thou shalt teach them ordinances and laws, and shalt shew them the way wherein they must walk, and the work that they must do.

chapters.zh: 并要从百姓中拣选有才能的人，就是敬畏神、诚实无妄、恨不义之财的人，派他们作千夫长、百夫长、五十夫长、十夫长，管理百姓，
chapters.en: Moreover thou shalt provide out of all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place over them, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens:

chapters.zh: 叫他们随时审判百姓，大事都要呈到你这里，小事他们自己可以审判。这样，你就轻省些，他们也可以同当此任。
chapters.en: And let them judge the people at all seasons: and it shall be, every great matter they shall bring unto thee, but every small matter they shall judge: so shall it be easier for thyself, and they shall bear with thee.

chapters.zh: 你若这样行，神也这样吩咐你，你就能受得住，这百姓也都平平安安归回他们的住处。
chapters.en: If thou shalt do this thing, and God command thee , then thou shalt be able to endure, and all this people shall also go to their place in peace.

chapters.zh: 于是，摩西听从他岳父的话，按著他所说的去行。
chapters.en: So Moses hearkened to the voice of his father in law, and did all that he had said.

chapters.zh: 摩西从以色列人中拣选了有才能的人，立他们为百姓的首领，作千夫长、百夫长、五十夫长、十夫长。
chapters.en: And Moses chose able men out of all Israel, and made them heads over the people, rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.

chapters.zh: 他们随时审判百姓，有难断的案件就呈到摩西那里，但各样小事他们自己审判。
chapters.en: And they judged the people at all seasons: the hard causes they brought unto Moses, but every small matter they judged themselves.

chapters.zh: 此后，摩西让他的岳父去，他就往本地去了。
chapters.en: And Moses let his father in law depart; and he went his way into his own land.

chapters.zh: 以色列人出埃及地以后，满了三个月的那一天，就来到西乃的旷野。
chapters.en: In the third month, when the children of Israel were gone forth out of the land of Egypt, the same day came they the wilderness of Sinai.

chapters.zh: 他们离了利非订，来到西乃的旷野，就在那里的山下安营。
chapters.en: For they were departed from Rephidim, and were come the desert of Sinai, and had pitched in the wilderness; and there Israel camped before the mount.

chapters.zh: 摩西到神那里，耶和华从山上呼唤他说：你要这样告诉雅各家，晓谕以色列人说：
chapters.en: And Moses went up unto God, and the LORD called unto him out of the mountain, saying, Thus shalt thou say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel;

chapters.zh: 我向埃及人所行的事，你们都看见了，且看见我如鹰将你们背在翅膀上，带来归我。
chapters.en: Ye have seen what I did unto the Egyptians, and I bare you on eagles' wings, and brought you unto myself.

chapters.zh: 如今你们若实在听从我的话，遵守我的约，就要在万民中作属我的子民，因为全地都是我的。
chapters.en: Now therefore, if ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me above all people: for all the earth mine:

chapters.zh: 你们要归我作祭司的国度，为圣洁的国民。这些话你要告诉以色列人。
chapters.en: And ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation. These the words which thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 摩西去召了民间的长老来，将耶和华所吩咐他的话都在他们面前陈明。
chapters.en: And Moses came and called for the elders of the people, and laid before their faces all these words which the LORD commanded him.

chapters.zh: 百姓都同声回答说：凡耶和华所说的，我们都要遵行。摩西就将百姓的话回覆耶和华。
chapters.en: And all the people answered together, and said, All that the LORD hath spoken we will do. And Moses returned the words of the people unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：我要在密云中临到你那里，叫百姓在我与你说话的时候可以听见，也可以永远信你了。于是，摩西将百姓的话奏告耶和华。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又对摩西说：你往百姓那里去，叫他们今天明天自洁，又叫他们洗衣服。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Go unto the people, and sanctify them to day and to morrow, and let them wash their clothes,

chapters.zh: 到第三天要预备好了，因为第三天耶和华要在众百姓眼前降临在西乃山上。
chapters.en: And be ready against the third day: for the third day the LORD will come down in the sight of all the people upon mount Sinai.

chapters.zh: 你要在山的四围给百姓定界限，说：你们当谨慎，不可上山去，也不可摸山的边界；凡摸这山的，必要治死他。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set bounds unto the people round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, go up into the mount, or touch the border of it: whosoever toucheth the mount shall be surely put to death:

chapters.zh: 不可用手摸他，必用石头打死，或用箭射透；无论是人是牲畜，都不得活。到角声拖长的时候，他们才可到山根来。
chapters.en: There shall not an hand touch it, but he shall surely be stoned, or shot through; whether beast or man, it shall not live: when the trumpet soundeth long, they shall come up to the mount.

chapters.zh: 摩西下山往百姓那里去，叫他们自洁，他们就洗衣服。
chapters.en: And Moses went down from the mount unto the people, and sanctified the people; and they washed their clothes.

chapters.zh: 他对百姓说：到第三天要预备好了。不可亲近女人。
chapters.en: And he said unto the people, Be ready against the third day: come not at wives.

chapters.zh: 到了第三天早晨，在山上有雷轰、闪电，和密云，并且角声甚大，营中的百姓尽都发颤。
chapters.en: And it came to pass on the third day in the morning, that there were thunders and lightnings, and a thick cloud upon the mount, and the voice of the trumpet exceeding loud; so that all the people that in the camp trembled.

chapters.zh: 摩西率领百姓出营迎接神，都站在山下。
chapters.en: And Moses brought forth the people out of the camp to meet with God; and they stood at the nether part of the mount.

chapters.zh: 西乃全山冒烟，因为耶和华在火中降于山上。山的烟气上腾，如烧窑一般，遍山大大的震动。
chapters.en: And mount Sinai was altogether on a smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire: and the smoke thereof ascended as the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.

chapters.zh: 角声渐渐的高而又高，摩西就说话，神有声音答应他。
chapters.en: And when the voice of the trumpet sounded long, and waxed louder and louder, Moses spake, and God answered him by a voice.

chapters.zh: 耶和华降临在西乃山顶上，耶和华召摩西上山顶，摩西就上去。
chapters.en: And the LORD came down upon mount Sinai, on the top of the mount: and the LORD called Moses to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你下去嘱咐百姓，不可闯过来到我面前观看，恐怕他们有多人死亡；
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Go down, charge the people, lest they break through unto the LORD to gaze, and many of them perish.

chapters.zh: 又叫亲近我的祭司自洁，恐怕我忽然出来击杀他们。
chapters.en: And let the priests also, which come near to the LORD, sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them.

chapters.zh: 摩西对耶和华说：百姓不能上西乃山，因为你已经嘱咐我们说：要在山的四围定界限，叫山成圣。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the LORD, The people cannot come up to mount Sinai: for thou chargedst us, saying, Set bounds about the mount, and sanctify it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对他说：下去罢，你要和亚伦一同上来；只是祭司和百姓不可闯过来上到我面前，恐怕我忽然出来击杀他们。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto him, Away, get thee down, and thou shalt come up, thou, and Aaron with thee: but let not the priests and the people break through to come up unto the LORD, lest he break forth upon them.

chapters.zh: 于是摩西下到百姓那里告诉他们。
chapters.en: So Moses went down unto the people, and spake unto them.

chapters.zh: 神吩咐这一切的话说：
chapters.en: And God spake all these words, saying,

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你的神，曾将你从埃及地为奴之家领出来。
chapters.en: I the LORD thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

chapters.zh: 除了我以外，你不可有别的神。
chapters.en: Thou shalt have no other gods before me.

chapters.zh: 不可为自己雕刻偶像，也不可做甚么形像彷佛上天、下地，和地底下、水中的百物。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image, or any likeness that in heaven above, or that in the earth beneath, or that in the water under the earth:

chapters.zh: 不可跪拜那些像，也不可事奉他，因为我耶和华 ─ 你的神是忌邪的神。恨我的，我必追讨他的罪，自父及子，直到三四代；
chapters.en: Thou shalt not bow down thyself to them, nor serve them: for I the LORD thy God a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth of them that hate me;

chapters.zh: 爱我、守我诫命的，我必向他们发慈爱，直到千代。
chapters.en: And shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me, and keep my commandments.

chapters.zh: 不可妄称耶和华 ─ 你神的名；因为妄称耶和华名的，耶和华必不以他为无罪。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain; for the LORD will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.

chapters.zh: 当记念安息日，守为圣日。
chapters.en: Remember the sabbath day, to keep it holy.

chapters.zh: 六日要劳碌做你一切的工，
chapters.en: Six days shalt thou labour, and do all thy work:

chapters.zh: 但第七日是向耶和华 ─ 你神当守的安息日。这一日你和你的儿女、仆婢、牲畜，并你城里寄居的客旅，无论何工都不可做；
chapters.en: But the seventh day the sabbath of the LORD thy God: thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thy cattle, nor thy stranger that within thy gates:

chapters.zh: 因为六日之内，耶和华造天、地、海，和其中的万物，第七日便安息，所以耶和华赐福与安息日，定为圣日。
chapters.en: For six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them , and rested the seventh day: wherefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day, and hallowed it.

chapters.zh: 当孝敬父母，使你的日子在耶和华 ─ 你神所赐你的地上得以长久。
chapters.en: Honour thy father and thy mother: that thy days may be long upon the land which the LORD thy God giveth thee.

chapters.zh: 不可杀人。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not kill.

chapters.zh: 不可奸淫。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

chapters.zh: 不可偷盗。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not steal.

chapters.zh: 不可作假见证陷害人。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbour.

chapters.zh: 不可贪恋人的房屋；也不可贪恋人的妻子、仆婢、牛驴，并他一切所有的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's house, thou shalt not covet thy neighbour's wife, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that thy neighbour's.

chapters.zh: 众百姓见雷轰、闪电、角声、山上冒烟，就都发颤，远远的站立，
chapters.en: And all the people saw the thunderings, and the lightnings, and the noise of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking: and when the people saw , they removed, and stood afar off.

chapters.zh: 对摩西说：求你和我们说话，我们必听；不要神和我们说话，恐怕我们死亡。
chapters.en: And they said unto Moses, Speak thou with us, and we will hear: but let not God speak with us, lest we die.

chapters.zh: 摩西对百姓说：不要惧怕；因为神降临是要试验你们，叫你们时常敬畏他，不致犯罪。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the people, Fear not: for God is come to prove you, and that his fear may be before your faces, that ye sin not.

chapters.zh: 于是百姓远远地站立；摩西就挨近神所在的幽暗之中。
chapters.en: And the people stood afar off, and Moses drew near unto the thick darkness where God .

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你要向以色列人这样说：你们自己看见我从天上和你们说话了。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Thus thou shalt say unto the children of Israel, Ye have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.

chapters.zh: 你们不可做甚么神像与我相配，不可为自己做金银的神像。
chapters.en: Ye shall not make with me gods of silver, neither shall ye make unto you gods of gold.

chapters.zh: 你要为我筑土坛，在上面以牛羊献为燔祭和平安祭。凡记下我名的地方，我必到那里赐福给你。
chapters.en: An altar of earth thou shalt make unto me, and shalt sacrifice thereon thy burnt offerings, and thy peace offerings, thy sheep, and thine oxen: in all places where I record my name I will come unto thee, and I will bless thee.

chapters.zh: 你若为我筑一座石坛，不可用凿成的石头，因你在上头一动家具，就把坛污秽了。
chapters.en: And if thou wilt make me an altar of stone, thou shalt not build it of hewn stone: for if thou lift up thy tool upon it, thou hast polluted it.

chapters.zh: 你上我的坛，不可用台阶，免得露出你的下体来。
chapters.en: Neither shalt thou go up by steps unto mine altar, that thy nakedness be not discovered thereon.

chapters.zh: 你在百姓面前所要立的典章是这样：
chapters.en: Now these the judgments which thou shalt set before them.

chapters.zh: 你若买希伯来人作奴仆，他必服事你六年；第七年他可以自由，白白的出去。
chapters.en: If thou buy an Hebrew servant, six years he shall serve: and in the seventh he shall go out free for nothing.

chapters.zh: 他若孤身来就可以孤身去；他若有妻，他的妻就可以同他出去。
chapters.en: If he came in by himself, he shall go out by himself: if he were married, then his wife shall go out with him.

chapters.zh: 他主人若给他妻子，妻子给他生了儿子或女儿，妻子和儿女要归主人，他要独自出去。
chapters.en: If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master's, and he shall go out by himself.

chapters.zh: 倘或奴仆明说：我爱我的主人和我的妻子儿女，不愿意自由出去。
chapters.en: And if the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free:

chapters.zh: 他的主人就要带他到审判官（审判官或作：神；下同）那里，又要带他到门前，靠近门框，用锥子穿他的耳朵，他就永远服事主人。
chapters.en: Then his master shall bring him unto the judges; he shall also bring him to the door, or unto the door post; and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul; and he shall serve him for ever.

chapters.zh: 人若卖女儿作婢女，婢女不可像男仆那样出去。
chapters.en: And if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do.

chapters.zh: 主人选定他归自己，若不喜欢他，就要许他赎身；主人既然用诡诈待他，就没有权柄卖给外邦人。
chapters.en: If she please not her master, who hath betrothed her to himself, then shall he let her be redeemed: to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power, seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her.

chapters.zh: 主人若选定他给自己的儿子，就当待他如同女儿。
chapters.en: And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.

chapters.zh: 若另娶一个，那女子的吃食、衣服，并好合的事，仍不可减少。
chapters.en: If he take him another ; her food, her raiment, and her duty of marriage, shall he not diminish.

chapters.zh: 若不向他行这三样，他就可以不用钱赎，白白的出去。
chapters.en: And if he do not these three unto her, then shall she go out free without money.

chapters.zh: 打人以致打死的，必要把他治死。
chapters.en: He that smiteth a man, so that he die, shall be surely put to death.

chapters.zh: 人若不是埋伏著杀人，乃是神交在他手中，我就设下一个地方，他可以往那里逃跑。
chapters.en: And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.

chapters.zh: 人若任意用诡计杀了他的邻舍，就是逃到我的坛那里，也当捉去把他治死。
chapters.en: But if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour, to slay him with guile; thou shalt take him from mine altar, that he may die.

chapters.zh: 打父母的，必要把他治死。
chapters.en: And he that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death.

chapters.zh: 拐带人口，或是把人卖了，或是留在他手下，必要把他治死。
chapters.en: And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 咒骂父母的，必要把他治死。
chapters.en: And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 人若彼此相争，这个用石头或是拳头打那个，尚且不至于死，不过躺卧在床，
chapters.en: And if men strive together, and one smite another with a stone, or with fist, and he die not, but keepeth bed:

chapters.zh: 若再能起来扶杖而出，那打他的可算无罪；但要将他耽误的工夫用钱赔补，并要将他全然医好。
chapters.en: If he rise again, and walk abroad upon his staff, then shall he that smote be quit: only he shall pay the loss of his time, and shall cause to be thoroughly healed.

chapters.zh: 人若用棍子打奴仆或婢女，立时死在他的手下，他必要受刑。
chapters.en: And if a man smite his servant, or his maid, with a rod, and he die under his hand; he shall be surely punished.

chapters.zh: 若过一两天才死，就可以不受刑，因为是用钱买的。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding, if he continue a day or two, he shall not be punished: for he his money.

chapters.zh: 人若彼此争斗，伤害有孕的妇人，甚至坠胎，随后却无别害，那伤害他的，总要按妇人的丈夫所要的，照审判官所断的，受罚。
chapters.en: If men strive, and hurt a woman with child, so that her fruit depart , and yet no mischief follow: he shall be surely punished, according as the woman's husband will lay upon him; and he shall pay as the judges .

chapters.zh: 若有别害，就要以命偿命，
chapters.en: And if mischief follow, then thou shalt give life for life,

chapters.zh: 以眼还眼，以牙还牙，以手还手，以脚还脚，
chapters.en: Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

chapters.zh: 以烙还烙，以伤还伤，以打还打。
chapters.en: Burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

chapters.zh: 人若打坏了他奴仆或是婢女的一只眼，就要因他的眼放他去得以自由。
chapters.en: And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye's sake.

chapters.zh: 若打掉了他奴仆或是婢女的一个牙，就要因他的牙放他去得以自由。
chapters.en: And if he smite out his manservant's tooth, or his maidservant's tooth; he shall let him go free for his tooth's sake.

chapters.zh: 牛若触死男人或是女人，总要用石头打死那牛，却不可吃他的肉；牛的主人可算无罪。
chapters.en: If an ox gore a man or a woman, that they die: then the ox shall be surely stoned, and his flesh shall not be eaten; but the owner of the ox quit.

chapters.zh: 倘若那牛素来是触人的，有人报告了牛主，他竟不把牛拴著，以致把男人或是女人触死，就要用石头打死那牛，牛主也必治死。
chapters.en: But if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past, and it hath been testified to his owner, and he hath not kept him in, but that he hath killed a man or a woman; the ox shall be stoned, and his owner also shall be put to death.

chapters.zh: 若罚他赎命的价银，他必照所罚的赎他的命。
chapters.en: If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.

chapters.zh: 牛无论触了人的儿子或是女儿，必照这例办理。
chapters.en: Whether he have gored a son, or have gored a daughter, according to this judgment shall it be done unto him.

chapters.zh: 牛若触了奴仆或是婢女，必将银子三十舍客勒给他们的主人，也要用石头把牛打死。
chapters.en: If the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant; he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver, and the ox shall be stoned.

chapters.zh: 人若敞著井口，或挖井不遮盖，有牛或驴掉在里头，
chapters.en: And if a man shall open a pit, or if a man shall dig a pit, and not cover it, and an ox or an ass fall therein;

chapters.zh: 井主要拿钱赔还本主人，死牲畜要归自己。
chapters.en: The owner of the pit shall make good, give money unto the owner of them; and the dead shall be his.

chapters.zh: 这人的牛若伤了那人的牛，以至于死，他们要卖了活牛，平分价值，也要平分死牛。
chapters.en: And if one man's ox hurt another's, that he die; then they shall sell the live ox, and divide the money of it; and the dead also they shall divide.

chapters.zh: 人若知道这牛素来是触人的，主人竟不把牛拴著，他必要以牛还牛，死牛要归自己。
chapters.en: Or if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time past, and his owner hath not kept him in; he shall surely pay ox for ox; and the dead shall be his own.

chapters.zh: 人若偷牛或羊，无论是宰了，是卖了，他就要以五牛赔一牛，四羊赔一羊。
chapters.en: If a man shall steal an ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it; he shall restore five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.

chapters.zh: 人若遇见贼挖窟窿，把贼打了，以至于死，就不能为他有流血的罪。
chapters.en: If a thief be found breaking up, and be smitten that he die, no blood for him.

chapters.zh: 若太阳已经出来，就为他有流血的罪。贼若被拿，总要赔还。若他一无所有，就要被卖，顶他所偷的物。
chapters.en: If the sun be risen upon him, blood for him; he should make full restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.

chapters.zh: 若他所偷的，或牛，或驴，或羊，仍在他手下存活，他就要加倍赔还。
chapters.en: If the theft be certainly found in his hand alive, whether it be ox, or ass, or sheep; he shall restore double.

chapters.zh: 人若在田间或在葡萄园里放牲畜，任凭牲畜上别人的田里去吃，就必拿自己田间上好的和葡萄园上好的赔还。
chapters.en: If a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten, and shall put in his beast, and shall feed in another man's field; of the best of his own field, and of the best of his own vineyard, shall he make restitution.

chapters.zh: 若点火焚烧荆棘，以致将别人堆积的禾捆，站著的禾稼，或是田园，都烧尽了，那点火的必要赔还。
chapters.en: If fire break out, and catch in thorns, so that the stacks of corn, or the standing corn, or the field, be consumed ; he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution.

chapters.zh: 人若将银钱或家具交付邻舍看守，这物从那人的家被偷去，若把贼找到了，贼要加倍赔还；
chapters.en: If a man shall deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man's house; if the thief be found, let him pay double.

chapters.zh: 若找不到贼，那家主必就近审判官，要看看他拿了原主的物件没有。
chapters.en: If the thief be not found, then the master of the house shall be brought unto the judges, whether he have put his hand unto his neighbour's goods.

chapters.zh: 两个人的案件，无论是为甚么过犯，或是为牛，为驴，为羊，为衣裳，或是为甚么失掉之物，有一人说：这是我的，两造就要将案件禀告审判官，审判官定谁有罪，谁就要加倍赔还。
chapters.en: For all manner of trespass, for ox, for ass, for sheep, for raiment, for any manner of lost thing, which challengeth to be his, the cause of both parties shall come before the judges; whom the judges shall condemn, he shall pay double unto his neighbour.

chapters.zh: 人若将驴，或牛，或羊，或别的牲畜，交付邻舍看守，牲畜或死，或受伤，或被赶去，无人看见，
chapters.en: If a man deliver unto his neighbour an ass, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast, to keep; and it die, or be hurt, or driven away, no man seeing :

chapters.zh: 那看守的人要凭著耶和华起誓，手里未曾拿邻舍的物，本主就要罢休，看守的人不必赔还。
chapters.en: shall an oath of the LORD be between them both, that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbour's goods; and the owner of it shall accept , and he shall not make good.

chapters.zh: 牲畜若从看守的那里被偷去，他就要赔还本主；
chapters.en: And if it be stolen from him, he shall make restitution unto the owner thereof.

chapters.zh: 若被野兽撕碎，看守的要带来当作证据，所撕的不必赔还。
chapters.en: If it be torn in pieces, let him bring it witness, he shall not make good that which was torn.

chapters.zh: 人若向邻舍借甚么，所借的或受伤，或死，本主没有同在一处，借的人总要赔还；
chapters.en: And if a man borrow of his neighbour, and it be hurt, or die, the owner thereof not with it, he shall surely make good.

chapters.zh: 若本主同在一处，他就不必赔还；若是雇的，也不必赔还，本是为雇价来的。
chapters.en: if the owner thereof with it, he shall not make good: if it an hired , it came for his hire.

chapters.zh: 人若引诱没有受聘的处女，与他行淫，他总要交出聘礼，娶他为妻。
chapters.en: And if a man entice a maid that is not betrothed, and lie with her, he shall surely endow her to be his wife.

chapters.zh: 若女子的父亲决不肯将女子给他，他就要按处女的聘礼，交出钱来。
chapters.en: If her father utterly refuse to give her unto him, he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins.

chapters.zh: 行邪术的女人，不可容他存活。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not suffer a witch to live.

chapters.zh: 凡与兽淫合的，总要把他治死。
chapters.en: Whosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 祭祀别神，不单单祭祀耶和华的，那人必要灭绝。
chapters.en: He that sacrificeth unto god, save unto the LORD only, he shall be utterly destroyed.

chapters.zh: 不可亏负寄居的，也不可欺压他，因为你们在埃及地也作过寄居的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt neither vex a stranger, nor oppress him: for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 不可苦待寡妇和孤儿；
chapters.en: Ye shall not afflict any widow, or fatherless child.

chapters.zh: 若是苦待他们一点，他们向我一哀求，我总要听他们的哀声，
chapters.en: If thou afflict them in any wise, and they cry at all unto me, I will surely hear their cry;

chapters.zh: 并要发烈怒，用刀杀你们，使你们的妻子为寡妇，儿女为孤儿。
chapters.en: And my wrath shall wax hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.

chapters.zh: 我民中有贫穷人与你同住，你若借钱给他，不可如放债的向他取利。
chapters.en: If thou lend money to my people poor by thee, thou shalt not be to him as an usurer, neither shalt thou lay upon him usury.

chapters.zh: 你即或拿邻舍的衣服作当头，必在日落以先归还他；
chapters.en: If thou at all take thy neighbour's raiment to pledge, thou shalt deliver it unto him by that the sun goeth down:

chapters.zh: 因他只有这一件当盖头，是他盖身的衣服，若是没有，他拿甚么睡觉呢？他哀求我，我就应允，因为我是有恩惠的。
chapters.en: For that his covering only, it his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I gracious.

chapters.zh: 不可毁谤神；也不可毁谤你百姓的官长。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not revile the gods, nor curse the ruler of thy people.

chapters.zh: 你要从你庄稼中的谷和酒醡中滴出来的酒拿来献上，不可迟延。你要将头生的儿子归给我。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not delay the first of thy ripe fruits, and of thy liquors: the firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me.

chapters.zh: 你牛羊头生的，也要这样；七天当跟著母，第八天要归给我。
chapters.en: Likewise shalt thou do with thine oxen, with thy sheep: seven days it shall be with his dam; on the eighth day thou shalt give it me.

chapters.zh: 你要在我面前为圣洁的人。因此，田间被野兽撕裂牲畜的肉，你们不可吃，要丢给狗吃。
chapters.en: And ye shall be holy men unto me: neither shall ye eat flesh torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it to the dogs.

chapters.zh: 不可随伙布散谣言；不可与恶人连手妄作见证。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not raise a false report: put not thine hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.

chapters.zh: 不可随众行恶；不可在争讼的事上随众偏行，作见证屈枉正直；
chapters.en: Thou shalt not follow a multitude to evil; neither shalt thou speak in a cause to decline after many to wrest :

chapters.zh: 也不可在争讼的事上偏护穷人。
chapters.en: Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause.

chapters.zh: 若遇见你仇敌的牛或驴失迷了路，总要牵回来交给他。
chapters.en: If thou meet thine enemy's ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again.

chapters.zh: 若看见恨你人的驴压卧在重驮之下，不可走开，务要和驴主一同抬开重驮。
chapters.en: If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, and wouldest forbear to help him, thou shalt surely help with him.

chapters.zh: 不可在穷人争讼的事上屈枉正直。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.

chapters.zh: 当远离虚假的事。不可杀无辜和有义的人，因我必不以恶人为义。
chapters.en: Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not: for I will not justify the wicked.

chapters.zh: 不可受贿赂；因为贿赂能叫明眼人变瞎了，又能颠倒义人的话。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take no gift: for the gift blindeth the wise, and perverteth the words of the righteous.

chapters.zh: 不可欺压寄居的；因为你们在埃及地作过寄居的，知道寄居的心。
chapters.en: Also thou shalt not oppress a stranger: for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 六年你要耕种田地，收藏土产，
chapters.en: And six years thou shalt sow thy land, and shalt gather in the fruits thereof:

chapters.zh: 只是第七年要叫地歇息，不耕不种，使你民中的穷人有吃的；他们所剩下的，野兽可以吃。你的葡萄园和橄榄园也要照样办理。
chapters.en: But the seventh thou shalt let it rest and lie still; that the poor of thy people may eat: and what they leave the beasts of the field shall eat. In like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard, with thy oliveyard.

chapters.zh: 六日你要做工，第七日要安息，使牛、驴可以歇息，并使你婢女的儿子和寄居的都可以舒畅。
chapters.en: Six days thou shalt do thy work, and on the seventh day thou shalt rest: that thine ox and thine ass may rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger, may be refreshed.

chapters.zh: 凡我对你们说的话，你们要谨守。别神的名，你不可题，也不可从你口中传说。
chapters.en: And in all that I have said unto you be circumspect: and make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth.

chapters.zh: 一年三次，你要向我守节。
chapters.en: Three times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year.

chapters.zh: 你要守除酵节，照我所吩咐你的，在亚笔月内所定的日期，吃无酵饼七天。谁也不可空手朝见我，因为你是这月出了埃及。
chapters.en: Thou shalt keep the feast of unleavened bread: (thou shalt eat unleavened bread seven days, as I commanded thee, in the time appointed of the month Abib; for in it thou camest out from Egypt: and none shall appear before me empty:)

chapters.zh: 又要守收割节，所收的是你田间所种、劳碌得来初熟之物。并在年底收藏，要守收藏节。
chapters.en: And the feast of harvest, the firstfruits of thy labours, which thou hast sown in the field: and the feast of ingathering, in the end of the year, when thou hast gathered in thy labours out of the field.

chapters.zh: 一切的男丁要一年三次朝见主耶和华。
chapters.en: Three times in the year all thy males shall appear before the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 不可将我祭牲的血和有酵的饼一同献上；也不可将我节上祭牲的脂油留到早晨。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread; neither shall the fat of my sacrifice remain until the morning.

chapters.zh: 地里首先初熟之物要送到耶和华 ─ 你神的殿。不可用山羊羔母的奶煮山羊羔。
chapters.en: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我差遣使者在你前面，在路上保护你，领你到我所预备的地方去。
chapters.en: Behold, I send an Angel before thee, to keep thee in the way, and to bring thee into the place which I have prepared.

chapters.zh: 他是奉我名来的；你们要在他面前谨慎，听从他的话，不可惹（惹或作：违背）他，因为他必不赦免你们的过犯。
chapters.en: Beware of him, and obey his voice, provoke him not; for he will not pardon your transgressions: for my name in him.

chapters.zh: 你若实在听从他的话，照著我一切所说的去行，我就向你的仇敌作仇敌，向你的敌人作敌人。
chapters.en: But if thou shalt indeed obey his voice, and do all that I speak; then I will be an enemy unto thine enemies, and an adversary unto thine adversaries.

chapters.zh: 我的使者要在你前面行，领你到亚摩利人、赫人、比利洗人、迦南人、希未人、耶布斯人那里去，我必将他们剪除。
chapters.en: For mine Angel shall go before thee, and bring thee in unto the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites: and I will cut them off.

chapters.zh: 你不可跪拜他们的神，不可事奉他，也不可效法他们的行为，却要把神像尽行拆毁，打碎他们的柱像。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do after their works: but thou shalt utterly overthrow them, and quite break down their images.

chapters.zh: 你们要事奉耶和华 ─ 你们的神，他必赐福与你的粮与你的水，也必从你们中间除去疾病。
chapters.en: And ye shall serve the LORD your God, and he shall bless thy bread, and thy water; and I will take sickness away from the midst of thee.

chapters.zh: 你境内必没有坠胎的，不生产的。我要使你满了你年日的数目。
chapters.en: There shall nothing cast their young, nor be barren, in thy land: the number of thy days I will fulfil.

chapters.zh: 凡你所到的地方，我要使那里的众民在你面前惊骇，扰乱，又要使你一切仇敌转背逃跑。
chapters.en: I will send my fear before thee, and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.

chapters.zh: 我要打发黄蜂飞在你前面，把希未人、迦南人、赫人撵出去。
chapters.en: And I will send hornets before thee, which shall drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before thee.

chapters.zh: 我不在一年之内将他们从你面前撵出去，恐怕地成为荒凉，野地的兽多起来害你。
chapters.en: I will not drive them out from before thee in one year; lest the land become desolate, and the beast of the field multiply against thee.

chapters.zh: 我要渐渐地将他们从你面前撵出去，等到你的人数加多，承受那地为业。
chapters.en: By little and little I will drive them out from before thee, until thou be increased, and inherit the land.

chapters.zh: 我要定你的境界，从红海直到非利士海，又从旷野直到大河。我要将那地的居民交在你手中，你要将他们从你面前撵出去。
chapters.en: And I will set thy bounds from the Red sea even unto the sea of the Philistines, and from the desert unto the river: for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand; and thou shalt drive them out before thee.

chapters.zh: 不可和他们并他们的神立约。
chapters.en: Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.

chapters.zh: 他们不可住在你的地上，恐怕他们使你得罪我。你若事奉他们的神，这必成为你的网罗。
chapters.en: They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against me: for if thou serve their gods, it will surely be a snare unto thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你和亚伦、拿答、亚比户，并以色列长老中的七十人，都要上到我这里来，远远的下拜。
chapters.en: And he said unto Moses, Come up unto the LORD, thou, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship ye afar off.

chapters.zh: 惟独你可以亲近耶和华；他们却不可亲近；百姓也不可和你一同上来。
chapters.en: And Moses alone shall come near the LORD: but they shall not come nigh; neither shall the people go up with him.

chapters.zh: 摩西下山，将耶和华的命令典章都述说与百姓听。众百姓齐声说：耶和华所吩咐的，我们都必遵行。
chapters.en: And Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD, and all the judgments: and all the people answered with one voice, and said, All the words which the LORD hath said will we do.

chapters.zh: 摩西将耶和华的命令都写上。清早起来，在山下筑一座坛，按以色列十二支派立十二根柱子，
chapters.en: And Moses wrote all the words of the LORD, and rose up early in the morning, and builded an altar under the hill, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Israel.

chapters.zh: 又打发以色列人中的少年人去献燔祭，又向耶和华献牛为平安祭。
chapters.en: And he sent young men of the children of Israel, which offered burnt offerings, and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 摩西将血一半盛在盆中，一半洒在坛上；
chapters.en: And Moses took half of the blood, and put in basons; and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.

chapters.zh: 又将约书念给百姓听。他们说：耶和华所吩咐的，我们都必遵行。
chapters.en: And he took the book of the covenant, and read in the audience of the people: and they said, All that the LORD hath said will we do, and be obedient.

chapters.zh: 摩西将血洒在百姓身上，说：你看！这是立约的血，是耶和华按这一切话与你们立约的凭据。
chapters.en: And Moses took the blood, and sprinkled on the people, and said, Behold the blood of the covenant, which the LORD hath made with you concerning all these words.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦、拿答、亚比户，并以色列长老中的七十人，都上了山。
chapters.en: Then went up Moses, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel:

chapters.zh: 他们看见以色列的神，他脚下彷佛有平铺的蓝宝石，如同天色明净。
chapters.en: And they saw the God of Israel: and under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone, and as it were the body of heaven in clearness.

chapters.zh: 他的手不加害在以色列的尊者身上。他们观看神；他们又吃又喝。
chapters.en: And upon the nobles of the children of Israel he laid not his hand: also they saw God, and did eat and drink.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你上山到我这里来，住在这里，我要将石版并我所写的律法和诫命赐给你，使你可以教训百姓。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Come up to me into the mount, and be there: and I will give thee tables of stone, and a law, and commandments which I have written; that thou mayest teach them.

chapters.zh: 摩西和他的帮手约书亚起来，上了神的山。
chapters.en: And Moses rose up, and his minister Joshua: and Moses went up into the mount of God.

chapters.zh: 摩西对长老说：你们在这里等著，等到我们再回来，有亚伦、户珥与你们同在。凡有争讼的，都可以就近他们去。
chapters.en: And he said unto the elders, Tarry ye here for us, until we come again unto you: and, behold, Aaron and Hur with you: if any man have any matters to do, let him come unto them.

chapters.zh: 摩西上山，有云彩把山遮盖。
chapters.en: And Moses went up into the mount, and a cloud covered the mount.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的荣耀停于西乃山；云彩遮盖山六天，第七天他从云中召摩西。
chapters.en: And the glory of the LORD abode upon mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days: and the seventh day he called unto Moses out of the midst of the cloud.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的荣耀在山顶上，在以色列人眼前，形状如烈火。
chapters.en: And the sight of the glory of the LORD like devouring fire on the top of the mount in the eyes of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 摩西进入云中上山，在山上四十昼夜。
chapters.en: And Moses went into the midst of the cloud, and gat him up into the mount: and Moses was in the mount forty days and forty nights.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你告诉以色列人当为我送礼物来；凡甘心乐意的，你们就可以收下归我。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me an offering: of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart ye shall take my offering.

chapters.zh: 所要收的礼物：就是金、银、铜，
chapters.en: And this the offering which ye shall take of them; gold, and silver, and brass,

chapters.zh: 蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，细麻，山羊毛，
chapters.en: And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' ,

chapters.zh: 染红的公羊皮，海狗皮，皂荚木，
chapters.en: And rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood,

chapters.zh: 点灯的油并做膏油和香的香料，
chapters.en: Oil for the light, spices for anointing oil, and for sweet incense,

chapters.zh: 红玛瑙与别样的宝石，可以镶嵌在以弗得和胸牌上。
chapters.en: Onyx stones, and stones to be set in the ephod, and in the breastplate.

chapters.zh: 又当为我造圣所，使我可以住在他们中间。
chapters.en: And let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them.

chapters.zh: 制造帐幕和其中的一切器具都要照我所指示你的样式。
chapters.en: According to all that I shew thee, the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all the instruments thereof, even so shall ye make .

chapters.zh: 要用皂荚木做一柜，长二肘半，宽一肘半，高一肘半。
chapters.en: And they shall make an ark shittim wood: two cubits and a half the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

chapters.zh: 要里外包上精金，四围镶上金牙边。
chapters.en: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, within and without shalt thou overlay it, and shalt make upon it a crown of gold round about.

chapters.zh: 也要铸四个金环，安在柜的四脚上；这边两环，那边两环。
chapters.en: And thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it, and put in the four corners thereof; and two rings in the one side of it, and two rings in the other side of it.

chapters.zh: 要用皂荚木做两根杠，用金包裹。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make staves shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.

chapters.zh: 要把杠穿在柜旁的环内，以便抬柜。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.

chapters.zh: 这杠要常在柜的环内，不可抽出来。
chapters.en: The staves shall be in the rings of the ark: they shall not be taken from it.

chapters.zh: 必将我所要赐给你的法版放在柜里。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee.

chapters.zh: 要用精金做施恩座（施恩：或作蔽罪；下同），长二肘半，宽一肘半。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make a mercy seat pure gold: two cubits and a half the length thereof, and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof.

chapters.zh: 要用金子锤出两个基路伯来，安在施恩座的两头。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make two cherubims gold, beaten work shalt thou make them, in the two ends of the mercy seat.

chapters.zh: 这头做一个基路伯，那头做一个基路伯，二基路伯要接连一块，在施恩座的两头。
chapters.en: And make one cherub on the one end, and the other cherub on the other end: of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims on the two ends thereof.

chapters.zh: 二基路伯要高张翅膀，遮掩施恩座。基路伯要脸对脸，朝著施恩座。
chapters.en: And the cherubims shall stretch forth wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, and their faces one to another; toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubims be.

chapters.zh: 要将施恩座安在柜的上边，又将我所要赐给你的法版放在柜里。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark; and in the ark thou shalt put the testimony that I shall give thee.

chapters.zh: 我要在那里与你相会，又要从法柜施恩座上二基路伯中间，和你说我所要吩咐你传给以色列人的一切事。
chapters.en: And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubims which upon the ark of the testimony, of all which I will give thee in commandment unto the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 要用皂荚木做一张桌子，长二肘，宽一肘，高一肘半。
chapters.en: Thou shalt also make a table shittim wood: two cubits the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof.

chapters.zh: 要包上精金，四围镶上金牙边。
chapters.en: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, and make thereto a crown of gold round about.

chapters.zh: 桌子的四围各做一掌宽的横梁，横梁上镶著金牙边。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make unto it a border of an hand breadth round about, and thou shalt make a golden crown to the border thereof round about.

chapters.zh: 要做四个金环，安在桌子的四角上，就是桌子四脚上的四角。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make for it four rings of gold, and put the rings in the four corners that on the four feet thereof.

chapters.zh: 安环子的地方要挨近横梁，可以穿杠抬桌子。
chapters.en: Over against the border shall the rings be for places of the staves to bear the table.

chapters.zh: 要用皂荚木做两根杠，用金包裹，以便抬桌子。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the staves shittim wood, and overlay them with gold, that the table may be borne with them.

chapters.zh: 要做桌子上的盘子、调羹，并奠酒的爵和瓶；这都要用精金制作。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the dishes thereof, and spoons thereof, and covers thereof, and bowls thereof, to cover withal: pure gold shalt thou make them.

chapters.zh: 又要在桌子上，在我面前，常摆陈设饼。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway.

chapters.zh: 要用精金做一个灯台。灯台的座和干与杯、球、花，都要接连一块锤出来。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make a candlestick pure gold: beaten work shall the candlestick be made: his shaft, and his branches, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, shall be of the same.

chapters.zh: 灯台两旁要杈出六个枝子：这旁三个，那旁三个。
chapters.en: And six branches shall come out of the sides of it; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side:

chapters.zh: 这旁每枝上有三个杯，形状像杏花，有球，有花；那旁每枝上也有三个杯，形状像杏花，有球，有花。从灯台杈出来的六个枝子都是如此。
chapters.en: Three bowls made like unto almonds, a knop and a flower in one branch; and three bowls made like almonds in the other branch, a knop and a flower: so in the six branches that come out of the candlestick.

chapters.zh: 灯台上有四个杯，形状像杏花，有球，有花。
chapters.en: And in the candlestick four bowls made like unto almonds, their knops and their flowers.

chapters.zh: 灯台每两个枝子以下有球与枝子接连一块。灯台出的六个枝子都是如此。
chapters.en: And a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick.

chapters.zh: 球和枝子要接连一块，都是一块精金锤出来的。
chapters.en: Their knops and their branches shall be of the same: all it one beaten work pure gold.

chapters.zh: 要做灯台的七个灯盏。祭司要点这灯，使灯光对照。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof: and they shall light the lamps thereof, that they may give light over against it.

chapters.zh: 灯台的蜡剪和蜡花盘也是要精金的。
chapters.en: And the tongs thereof, and the snuffdishes thereof, pure gold.

chapters.zh: 做灯台和这一切的器具要用精金一他连得。
chapters.en: a talent of pure gold shall he make it, with all these vessels.

chapters.zh: 要谨慎做这些物件，都要照著在山上指示你的样式。
chapters.en: And look that thou make after their pattern, which was shewed thee in the mount.

chapters.zh: 你要用十幅幔子做帐幕。这些幔子要用撚的细麻和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线制造，并用巧匠的手工绣上基路伯。
chapters.en: Moreover thou shalt make the tabernacle ten curtains fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them.

chapters.zh: 每幅幔子要长二十八肘，宽四肘，幔子都要一样的尺寸。
chapters.en: The length of one curtain eight and twenty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and every one of the curtains shall have one measure.

chapters.zh: 这五幅幔子要幅幅相连；那五幅幔子也要幅幅相连。
chapters.en: The five curtains shall be coupled together one to another; and five curtains coupled one to another.

chapters.zh: 在这相连的幔子末幅边上要做蓝色的钮扣；在那相连的幔子末幅边上也要照样做。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make loops of blue upon the edge of the one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling; and likewise shalt thou make in the uttermost edge of curtain, in the coupling of the second.

chapters.zh: 要在这相连的幔子上做五十个钮扣；在那相连的幔子上也做五十个钮扣，都要两两相对。
chapters.en: Fifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain, and fifty loops shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain that in the coupling of the second; that the loops may take hold one of another.

chapters.zh: 又要做五十个金钩，用钩使幔子相连，这才成了一个帐幕。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make fifty taches of gold, and couple the curtains together with the taches: and it shall be one tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 你要用山羊毛织十一幅幔子，作为帐幕以上的罩棚。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make curtains goats' to be a covering upon the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make.

chapters.zh: 每幅幔子要长三十肘，宽四肘；十一幅幔子都要一样的尺寸。
chapters.en: The length of one curtain thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: and the eleven curtains of one measure.

chapters.zh: 要把五幅幔子连成一幅，又把六幅幔子连成一幅，这第六幅幔子要在罩棚的前面折上去。
chapters.en: And thou shalt couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shalt double the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 在这相连的幔子末幅边上要做五十个钮扣；在那相连的幔子末幅边上也做五十个钮扣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain outmost in the coupling, and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.

chapters.zh: 又要做五十个铜钩，钩在钮扣中，使罩棚连成一个。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make fifty taches of brass, and put the taches into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.

chapters.zh: 罩棚的幔子所余那垂下来的半幅幔子，要垂在帐幕的后头。
chapters.en: And the remnant that remaineth of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remaineth, shall hang over the backside of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 罩棚的幔子所余长的，这边一肘，那边一肘，要垂在帐幕的两旁，遮盖帐幕。
chapters.en: And a cubit on the one side, and a cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent, it shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.

chapters.zh: 又要用染红的公羊皮做罩棚的盖；再用海狗皮做一层罩棚上的顶盖。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make a covering for the tent rams' skins dyed red, and a covering above badgers' skins.

chapters.zh: 你要用皂荚木做帐幕的竖板。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle shittim wood standing up.

chapters.zh: 每块要长十肘，宽一肘半；
chapters.en: Ten cubits the length of a board, and a cubit and a half the breadth of one board.

chapters.zh: 每块必有两榫相对。帐幕一切的板都要这样做。
chapters.en: Two tenons in one board, set in order one against another: thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 帐幕的南面要做板二十块。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards on the south side southward.

chapters.zh: 在这二十块板底下要做四十个带卯的银座，两卯接这块板上的两榫，两卯接那块板上的两榫。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.

chapters.zh: 帐幕第二面，就是北面，也要做板二十块
chapters.en: And for the second side of the tabernacle on the north side twenty boards:

chapters.zh: 和带卯的银座四十个；这板底下有两卯，那板底下也有两卯。
chapters.en: And their forty sockets silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

chapters.zh: 帐幕的后面，就是西面，要做板六块。
chapters.en: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards.

chapters.zh: 帐幕后面的拐角要做板两块。
chapters.en: And two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.

chapters.zh: 板的下半截要双的，上半截要整的，直顶到第一个环子；两块都要这样做两个拐角。
chapters.en: And they shall be coupled together beneath, and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.

chapters.zh: 必有八块板和十六个带卯的银座；这板底下有两卯，那板底下也有两卯。
chapters.en: And they shall be eight boards, and their sockets silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

chapters.zh: 你要用皂荚木做闩：为帐幕这面的板做五闩，
chapters.en: And thou shalt make bars shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

chapters.zh: 为帐幕那面的板做五闩，又为帐幕后面的板做五闩。
chapters.en: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the two sides westward.

chapters.zh: 板腰间的中闩要从这一头通到那一头。
chapters.en: And the middle bar in the midst of the boards shall reach from end to end.

chapters.zh: 板要用金子包裹，又要做板上的金环套闩；闩也要用金子包裹。
chapters.en: And thou shalt overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings gold places for the bars: and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold.

chapters.zh: 要照著在山上指示你的样式立起帐幕。
chapters.en: And thou shalt rear up the tabernacle according to the fashion thereof which was shewed thee in the mount.

chapters.zh: 你要用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和撚的细麻织幔子，以巧匠的手工绣上基路伯。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make a vail blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen of cunning work: with cherubims shall it be made:

chapters.zh: 要把幔子挂在四根包金的皂荚木柱子上，柱子上当有金钩，柱子安在四个带卯的银座上。
chapters.en: And thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of shittim overlaid with gold: their hooks gold, upon the four sockets of silver.

chapters.zh: 要使幔子垂在钩子下，把法柜抬进幔子内；这幔子要将圣所和至圣所隔开。
chapters.en: And thou shalt hang up the vail under the taches, that thou mayest bring in thither within the vail the ark of the testimony: and the vail shall divide unto you between the holy and the most holy.

chapters.zh: 又要把施恩座安在至圣所内的法柜上，
chapters.en: And thou shalt put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy .

chapters.zh: 把桌子安在幔子外帐幕的北面；把灯台安在帐幕的南面，彼此相对。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set the table without the vail, and the candlestick over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and thou shalt put the table on the north side.

chapters.zh: 你要拿蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和撚的细麻，用绣花的手工织帐幕的门帘。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make an hanging for the door of the tent, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework.

chapters.zh: 要用皂荚木为帘子做五根柱子，用金子包裹。柱子上当有金钩；又要为柱子用铜铸造五个带卯的座。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make for the hanging five pillars shittim , and overlay them with gold, their hooks gold: and thou shalt cast five sockets of brass for them.

chapters.zh: 你要用皂荚木做坛。这坛要四方的，长五肘，宽五肘，高三肘。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make an altar shittim wood, five cubits long, and five cubits broad; the altar shall be foursquare: and the height thereof three cubits.

chapters.zh: 要在坛的四拐角上做四个角，与坛接连一块，用铜把坛包裹。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the horns of it upon the four corners thereof: his horns shall be of the same: and thou shalt overlay it with brass.

chapters.zh: 要做盆，收去坛上的灰，又做铲子、盘子、肉锸子、火鼎；坛上一切的器具都用铜做。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make his pans to receive his ashes, and his shovels, and his basons, and his fleshhooks, and his firepans: all the vessels thereof thou shalt make brass.

chapters.zh: 要为坛做一个铜网，在网的四角上做四个铜环，
chapters.en: And thou shalt make for it a grate of network brass; and upon the net shalt thou make four brasen rings in the four corners thereof.

chapters.zh: 把网安在坛四面的围腰板以下，使网从下达到坛的半腰。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put it under the compass of the altar beneath, that the net may be even to the midst of the altar.

chapters.zh: 又要用皂荚木为坛做杠，用铜包裹。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make staves for the altar, staves shittim wood, and overlay them with brass.

chapters.zh: 这杠要穿在坛两旁的环子内，用以抬坛。
chapters.en: And the staves shall be put into the rings, and the staves shall be upon the two sides of the altar, to bear it.

chapters.zh: 要用板做坛，坛是空的，都照著在山上指示你的样式作。
chapters.en: Hollow with boards shalt thou make it: as it was shewed thee in the mount, so shall they make .

chapters.zh: 你要做帐幕的院子。院子的南面要用撚的细麻做帷子，长一百肘。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward hangings for the court fine twined linen of an hundred cubits long for one side:

chapters.zh: 帷子的柱子要二十根，带卯的铜座二十个。柱子上的钩子和杆子都要用银子做。
chapters.en: And the twenty pillars thereof and their twenty sockets brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

chapters.zh: 北面也当有帷子，长一百肘，帷子的柱子二十根，带卯的铜座二十个。柱子上的钩子和杆子都要用银子做。
chapters.en: And likewise for the north side in length hangings of an hundred long, and his twenty pillars and their twenty sockets brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

chapters.zh: 院子的西面当有帷子，宽五十肘，帷子的柱子十根，带卯的座十个。
chapters.en: And the breadth of the court on the west side hangings of fifty cubits: their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.

chapters.zh: 院子的东面要宽五十肘。
chapters.en: And the breadth of the court on the east side eastward fifty cubits.

chapters.zh: 门这边的帷子要十五肘，帷子的柱子三根，带卯的座三个。
chapters.en: The hangings of one side fifteen cubits: their pillars three, and their sockets three.

chapters.zh: 门那边的帷子也要十五肘，帷子的柱子三根，带卯的座三个。
chapters.en: And on the other side hangings fifteen : their pillars three, and their sockets three.

chapters.zh: 院子的门当有帘子，长二十肘，要拿蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和撚的细麻，用绣花的手工织成，柱子四根，带卯的座四个。
chapters.en: And for the gate of the court an hanging of twenty cubits, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, wrought with needlework: their pillars four, and their sockets four.

chapters.zh: 院子四围一切的柱子都要用银杆连络，柱子上的钩子要用银做，带卯的座要用铜做。
chapters.en: All the pillars round about the court filleted with silver; their hooks silver, and their sockets brass.

chapters.zh: 院子要长一百肘，宽五十肘，高五肘，帷子要用撚的细麻做，带卯的座要用铜做。
chapters.en: The length of the court an hundred cubits, and the breadth fifty every where, and the height five cubits fine twined linen, and their sockets brass.

chapters.zh: 帐幕各样用处的器具，并帐幕一切的橛子，和院子里一切的橛子，都要用铜做。
chapters.en: All the vessels of the tabernacle in all the service thereof, and all the pins thereof, and all the pins of the court, brass.

chapters.zh: 你要吩咐以色列人，把那为点灯捣成的清橄榄油拿来给你，使灯常常点著。
chapters.en: And thou shalt command the children of Israel, that they bring thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamp to burn always.

chapters.zh: 在会幕中法柜前的幔外，亚伦和他的儿子，从晚上到早晨，要在耶和华面前经理这灯。这要作以色列人世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: In the tabernacle of the congregation without the vail, which before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall order it from evening to morning before the LORD: a statute for ever unto their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 你要从以色列人中，使你的哥哥亚伦和他的儿子拿答、亚比户、以利亚撒、以他玛一同就近你，给我供祭司的职分。
chapters.en: And take thou unto thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office, Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron's sons.

chapters.zh: 你要给你哥哥亚伦做圣衣为荣耀，为华美。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make holy garments for Aaron thy brother for glory and for beauty.

chapters.zh: 又要吩咐一切心中有智慧的，就是我用智慧的灵所充满的，给亚伦做衣服，使他分别为圣，可以给我供祭司的职分。
chapters.en: And thou shalt speak unto all wise hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they may make Aaron's garments to consecrate him, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 所要做的就是胸牌、以弗得、外袍、杂色的内袍、冠冕、腰带，使你哥哥亚伦和他儿子穿这圣服，可以给我供祭司的职分。
chapters.en: And these the garments which they shall make; a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a broidered coat, a mitre, and a girdle: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto me in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 要用金线和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并细麻去做。
chapters.en: And they shall take gold, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen.

chapters.zh: 他们要拿金线和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻，用巧匠的手工做以弗得。
chapters.en: And they shall make the ephod gold, blue, and purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cunning work.

chapters.zh: 以弗得当有两条肩带，接上两头，使他相连。
chapters.en: It shall have the two shoulderpieces thereof joined at the two edges thereof; and it shall be joined together.

chapters.zh: 其上巧工织的带子，要和以弗得一样的做法，用以束上，与以弗得接连一块，要用金线和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻做成。
chapters.en: And the curious girdle of the ephod, which upon it, shall be of the same, according to the work thereof; gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.

chapters.zh: 要取两块红玛瑙，在上面刻以色列儿子的名字：
chapters.en: And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and grave on them the names of the children of Israel:

chapters.zh: 六个名字在这块宝石上，六个名字在那块宝石上，都照他们生来的次序。
chapters.en: Six of their names on one stone, and six names of the rest on the other stone, according to their birth.

chapters.zh: 要用刻宝石的手工，彷佛刻图书，按著以色列儿子的名字，刻这两块宝石，要镶在金槽上。
chapters.en: With the work of an engraver in stone, the engravings of a signet, shalt thou engrave the two stones with the names of the children of Israel: thou shalt make them to be set in ouches of gold.

chapters.zh: 要将这两块宝石安在以弗得的两条肩带上，为以色列人做纪念石。亚伦要在两肩上担他们的名字，在耶和华面前作为纪念。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulders of the ephod stones of memorial unto the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD upon his two shoulders for a memorial.

chapters.zh: 要用金子做二槽，
chapters.en: And thou shalt make ouches gold;

chapters.zh: 又拿精金，用拧工彷佛拧绳子，做两条炼子，把这拧成的炼子搭在二槽上。
chapters.en: And two chains pure gold at the ends; wreathen work shalt thou make them, and fasten the wreathen chains to the ouches.

chapters.zh: 你要用巧匠的手工做一个决断的胸牌。要和以弗得一样的做法：用金线和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻做成。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the breastplate of judgment with cunning work; after the work of the ephod thou shalt make it; gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, shalt thou make it.

chapters.zh: 这胸牌要四方的，叠为两层，长一虎口，宽一虎口。
chapters.en: Foursquare it shall be doubled; a span the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof.

chapters.zh: 要在上面镶宝石四行：第一行是红宝石、红璧玺、红玉；
chapters.en: And thou shalt set in it settings of stones, four rows of stones: row a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle: the first row.

chapters.zh: 第二行是绿宝石、蓝宝石、金钢石；
chapters.en: And the second row an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

chapters.zh: 第三行是紫玛瑙、白玛瑙、紫晶；
chapters.en: And the third row a ligure, an agate, and an amethyst.

chapters.zh: 第四行是水苍玉、红玛瑙、碧玉。这都要镶在金槽中。
chapters.en: And the fourth row a beryl, and an onyx, and a jasper: they shall be set in gold in their inclosings.

chapters.zh: 这些宝石都要按著以色列十二个儿子的名字，彷佛刻图书，刻十二个支派的名字。
chapters.en: And the stones shall be with the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, the engravings of a signet; every one with his name shall they be according to the twelve tribes.

chapters.zh: 要在胸牌上用精金拧成如绳的炼子。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make upon the breastplate chains at the ends wreathen work pure gold.

chapters.zh: 在胸牌上也要做两个金环，安在胸牌的两头。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make upon the breastplate two rings of gold, and shalt put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.

chapters.zh: 要把那两条拧成的金炼子，穿过胸牌两头的环子。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put the two wreathen of gold in the two rings on the ends of the breastplate.

chapters.zh: 又要把炼子的那两头接在两槽上，安在以弗得前面肩带上。
chapters.en: And two ends of the two wreathen thou shalt fasten in the two ouches, and put on the shoulderpieces of the ephod before it.

chapters.zh: 要做两个金环，安在胸牌的两头，在以弗得里面的边上。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and thou shalt put them upon the two ends of the breastplate in the border thereof, which in the side of the ephod inward.

chapters.zh: 又要做两个金环，安在以弗得前面两条肩带的下边，挨近相接之处，在以弗得巧工织的带子以上。
chapters.en: And two rings of gold thou shalt make, and shalt put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath, toward the forepart thereof, over against the coupling thereof, above the curious girdle of the ephod.

chapters.zh: 要用蓝细带子把胸牌的环子与以弗得的环子系住，使胸牌贴在以弗得巧工织的带子上，不可与以弗得离缝。
chapters.en: And they shall bind the breastplate by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that may be above the curious girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.

chapters.zh: 亚伦进圣所的时候，要将决断胸牌，就是刻著以色列儿子名字的，带在胸前，在耶和华面前常作纪念。
chapters.en: And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart, when he goeth in unto the holy , for a memorial before the LORD continually.

chapters.zh: 又要将乌陵和土明放在决断的胸牌里；亚伦进到耶和华面前的时候，要带在胸前，在耶和华面前常将以色列人的决断牌带在胸前。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be upon Aaron's heart, when he goeth in before the LORD: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the LORD continually.

chapters.zh: 你要做以弗得的外袍，颜色全是蓝的。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the robe of the ephod all blue.

chapters.zh: 袍上要为头留一领口，口的周围织出领边来，彷佛铠甲的领口，免得破裂。
chapters.en: And there shall be an hole in the top of it, in the midst thereof: it shall have a binding of woven work round about the hole of it, as it were the hole of an habergeon, that it be not rent.

chapters.zh: 袍子周围底边上要用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线做石榴。在袍子周围的石榴中间要有金铃铛：
chapters.en: And upon the hem of it thou shalt make pomegranates blue, and purple, and scarlet, round about the hem thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:

chapters.zh: 一个金铃铛一个石榴，一个金铃铛一个石榴，在袍子周围的底边上。
chapters.en: A golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, upon the hem of the robe round about.

chapters.zh: 亚伦供职的时候要穿这袍子。他进圣所到耶和华面前，以及出来的时候，袍上的响声必被听见，使他不至于死亡。
chapters.en: And it shall be upon Aaron to minister: and his sound shall be heard when he goeth in unto the holy before the LORD, and when he cometh out, that he die not.

chapters.zh: 你要用精金做一面牌，在上面按刻图书之法刻著归耶和华为圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make a plate pure gold, and grave upon it, the engravings of a signet, HOLINESS TO THE LORD.

chapters.zh: 要用一条蓝细带子将牌系在冠冕的前面。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put it on a blue lace, that it may be upon the mitre; upon the forefront of the mitre it shall be.

chapters.zh: 这牌必在亚伦的额上，亚伦要担当干犯圣物条例的罪孽；这圣物是以色列人在一切的圣礼物上所分别为圣的。这牌要常在他的额上，使他们可以在耶和华面前蒙悦纳。
chapters.en: And it shall be upon Aaron's forehead, that Aaron may bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 要用杂色细麻线织内袍，用细麻布做冠冕，又用绣花的手工做腰带。
chapters.en: And thou shalt embroider the coat of fine linen, and thou shalt make the mitre fine linen, and thou shalt make the girdle needlework.

chapters.zh: 你要为亚伦的儿子做内袍、腰带、裹头巾，为荣耀，为华美。
chapters.en: And for Aaron's sons thou shalt make coats, and thou shalt make for them girdles, and bonnets shalt thou make for them, for glory and for beauty.

chapters.zh: 要把这些给你的哥哥亚伦和他的儿子穿戴，又要膏他们，将他们分别为圣，好给我供祭司的职分。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 要给他们做细麻布裤子，遮掩下体；裤子当从腰达到大腿。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover their nakedness; from the loins even unto the thighs they shall reach:

chapters.zh: 亚伦和他儿子进入会幕，或就近坛，在圣所供职的时候必穿上，免得担罪而死。这要为亚伦和他的后裔作永远的定例。
