chapters.en: And they shall be upon Aaron, and upon his sons, when they come in unto the tabernacle of the congregation, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy ; that they bear not iniquity, and die: a statute for ever unto him and his seed after him.

chapters.zh: 你使亚伦和他儿子成圣，给我供祭司的职分，要如此行：取一只公牛犊，两只无残疾的公绵羊，
chapters.en: And this the thing that thou shalt do unto them to hallow them, to minister unto me in the priest's office: Take one young bullock, and two rams without blemish,

chapters.zh: 无酵饼和调油的无酵饼，与抹油的无酵薄饼；这都要用细麦面做成。
chapters.en: And unleavened bread, and cakes unleavened tempered with oil, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil: wheaten flour shalt thou make them.

chapters.zh: 这饼要装在一个筐子里，连筐子带来，又把公牛和两只公绵羊牵来。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the bullock and the two rams.

chapters.zh: 要使亚伦和他儿子到会幕门口来，用水洗身。
chapters.en: And Aaron and his sons thou shalt bring unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and shalt wash them with water.

chapters.zh: 要给亚伦穿上内袍和以弗得的外袍，并以弗得，又带上胸牌，束上以弗得巧工织的带子。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take the garments, and put upon Aaron the coat, and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and gird him with the curious girdle of the ephod:

chapters.zh: 把冠冕戴在他头上，将圣冠加在冠冕上，
chapters.en: And thou shalt put the mitre upon his head, and put the holy crown upon the mitre.

chapters.zh: 就把膏油倒在他头上膏他。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour upon his head, and anoint him.

chapters.zh: 要叫他的儿子来，给他们穿上内袍。
chapters.en: And thou shalt bring his sons, and put coats upon them.

chapters.zh: 给亚伦和他儿子束上腰带，包上裹头巾，他们就凭永远的定例得了祭司的职任。又要将亚伦和他儿子分别为圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt gird them with girdles, Aaron and his sons, and put the bonnets on them: and the priest's office shall be theirs for a perpetual statute: and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.

chapters.zh: 你要把公牛带到会幕前，亚伦和他儿子要按手在公牛的头上。
chapters.en: And thou shalt cause a bullock to be brought before the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the bullock.

chapters.zh: 你要在耶和华面前，在会幕门口，宰这公牛。
chapters.en: And thou shalt kill the bullock before the LORD, the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 要取些公牛的血，用指头抹在坛的四角上，把血都倒在坛脚那里。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and put upon the horns of the altar with thy finger, and pour all the blood beside the bottom of the altar.

chapters.zh: 要把一切盖脏的脂油与肝上的网子，并两个腰子和腰子上的脂油，都烧在坛上。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that upon them, and burn upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 只是公牛的皮、肉、粪都要用火烧在营外。这牛是赎罪祭。
chapters.en: But the flesh of the bullock, and his skin, and his dung, shalt thou burn with fire without the camp: it a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 你要牵一只公绵羊来，亚伦和他儿子要按手在这羊的头上。
chapters.en: Thou shalt also take one ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.

chapters.zh: 要宰这羊，把血洒在坛的周围。
chapters.en: And thou shalt slay the ram, and thou shalt take his blood, and sprinkle round about upon the altar.

chapters.zh: 要把羊切成块子，洗净五脏和腿，连块子带头，都放在一处。
chapters.en: And thou shalt cut the ram in pieces, and wash the inwards of him, and his legs, and put unto his pieces, and unto his head.

chapters.zh: 要把全羊烧在坛上，是给耶和华献的燔祭，是献给耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And thou shalt burn the whole ram upon the altar: it a burnt offering unto the LORD: it a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你要将那一只公绵羊牵来，亚伦和他儿子要按手在羊的头上。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram.

chapters.zh: 你要宰这羊，取点血抹在亚伦的右耳垂上和他儿子的右耳垂上，又抹在他们右手的大拇指上和右脚的大拇指上；并要把血洒在坛的四围。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou kill the ram, and take of his blood, and put upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot, and sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.

chapters.zh: 你要取点膏油和坛上的血，弹在亚伦和他的衣服上，并他儿子和他儿子的衣服上，他们和他们的衣服就一同成圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take of the blood that upon the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon the garments of his sons with him: and he shall be hallowed, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons' garments with him.

chapters.zh: 你要取这羊的脂油和肥尾巴，并盖脏的脂油与肝上的网子，两个腰子和腰子上的脂油并右腿（这是承接圣职所献的羊）。
chapters.en: Also thou shalt take of the ram the fat and the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards, and the caul the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that upon them, and the right shoulder; for it a ram of consecration:

chapters.zh: 再从耶和华面前装无酵饼的筐子中取一个饼，一个调油的饼和一个薄饼，
chapters.en: And one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of the unleavened bread that before the LORD:

chapters.zh: 都放在亚伦的手上和他儿子的手上，作为摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put all in the hands of Aaron, and in the hands of his sons; and shalt wave them a wave offering before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 要从他们手中接过来，烧在耶和华面前坛上的燔祭上，是献给耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And thou shalt receive them of their hands, and burn upon the altar for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour before the LORD: it an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你要取亚伦承接圣职所献公羊的胸，作为摇祭，在耶和华面前摇一摇，这就可以作你的分。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take the breast of the ram of Aaron's consecration, and wave it a wave offering before the LORD: and it shall be thy part.

chapters.zh: 那摇祭的胸和举祭的腿，就是承接圣职所摇的、所举的，是归亚伦和他儿子的。这些你都要成为圣，
chapters.en: And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of the consecration, of which for Aaron, and of which is for his sons:

chapters.zh: 作亚伦和他子孙从以色列人中永远所得的分，因为是举祭。这要从以色列人的平安祭中，作为献给耶和华的举祭。
chapters.en: And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings, their heave offering unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 亚伦的圣衣要留给他的子孙，可以穿著受膏，又穿著承接圣职。
chapters.en: And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.

chapters.zh: 他的子孙接续他当祭司的，每逢进会幕在圣所供职的时候，要穿七天。
chapters.en: that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy .

chapters.zh: 你要将承接圣职所献公羊的肉煮在圣处。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and seethe his flesh in the holy place.

chapters.zh: 亚伦和他儿子要在会幕门口吃这羊的肉和筐内的饼。
chapters.en: And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that in the basket, the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 他们吃那些赎罪之物，好承接圣职，使他们成圣；只是外人不可吃，因为这是圣物。
chapters.en: And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat , because they holy.

chapters.zh: 那承接圣职所献的肉或饼，若有一点留到早晨，就要用火烧了，不可吃这物，因为是圣物。
chapters.en: And if ought of the flesh of the consecrations, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it holy.

chapters.zh: 你要这样照我一切所吩咐的，向亚伦和他儿子行承接圣职的礼七天。
chapters.en: And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all which I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.

chapters.zh: 每天要献公牛一只为赎罪祭。你洁净坛的时候，坛就洁净了；且要用膏抹坛，使坛成圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt offer every day a bullock a sin offering for atonement: and thou shalt cleanse the altar, when thou hast made an atonement for it, and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.

chapters.zh: 要洁净坛七天，使坛成圣，坛就成为至圣。凡挨著坛的都成为圣。
chapters.en: Seven days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar, and sanctify it; and it shall be an altar most holy: whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 你每天所要献在坛上的就是两只一岁的羊羔；
chapters.en: Now this which thou shalt offer upon the altar; two lambs of the first year day by day continually.

chapters.zh: 早晨要献这一只，黄昏的时候要献那一只。
chapters.en: The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at even:

chapters.zh: 和这一只羊羔同献的，要用细面伊法十分之一与捣成的油一欣四分之一调和，又用酒一欣四分之一作为奠祭。
chapters.en: And with the one lamb a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of an hin of wine a drink offering.

chapters.zh: 那一只羊羔要在黄昏的时候献上，照著早晨的素祭和奠祭的礼办理，作为献给耶和华馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering of the morning, and according to the drink offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 这要在耶和华面前、会幕门口，作你们世世代代常献的燔祭。我要在那里与你们相会，和你们说话。
chapters.en: a continual burnt offering throughout your generations the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD: where I will meet you, to speak there unto thee.

chapters.zh: 我要在那里与以色列人相会，会幕就要因我的荣耀成为圣。
chapters.en: And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and shall be sanctified by my glory.

chapters.zh: 我要使会幕和坛成圣，也要使亚伦和他的儿子成圣，给我供祭司的职分。
chapters.en: And I will sanctify the tabernacle of the congregation, and the altar: I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons, to minister to me in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 我要住在以色列人中间，作他们的神。
chapters.en: And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.

chapters.zh: 他们必知道我是耶和华 ─ 他们的神，是将他们从埃及地领出来的，为要住在他们中间。我是耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: And they shall know that I the LORD their God, that brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them: I the LORD their God.

chapters.zh: 你要用皂荚木做一座烧香的坛。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: shittim wood shalt thou make it.

chapters.zh: 这坛要四方的，长一肘，宽一肘，高二肘；坛的四角要与坛接连一块。
chapters.en: A cubit the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits the height thereof: the horns thereof of the same.

chapters.zh: 要用精金把坛的上面与坛的四围，并坛的四角，包裹；又要在坛的四围镶上金牙边。
chapters.en: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.

chapters.zh: 要做两个金环安在牙子边以下，在坛的两旁，两根横撑上，作为穿杠的用处，以便抬坛。
chapters.en: And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make ; and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal.

chapters.zh: 要用皂荚木做杠，用金包裹。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make the staves shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.

chapters.zh: 要把坛放在法柜前的幔子外，对著法柜上的施恩座，就是我要与你相会的地方。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put it before the vail that by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.

chapters.zh: 亚伦在坛上要烧馨香料做的香；每早晨他收拾灯的时候，要烧这香。
chapters.en: And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it.

chapters.zh: 黄昏点灯的时候，他要在耶和华面前烧这香，作为世世代代常烧的香。
chapters.en: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations.

chapters.zh: 在这坛上不可奉上异样的香，不可献燔祭、素祭，也不可浇上奠祭。
chapters.en: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.

chapters.zh: 亚伦一年一次要在坛的角上行赎罪之礼。他一年一次要用赎罪祭牲的血在坛上行赎罪之礼，作为世世代代的定例。这坛在耶和华面前为至圣。
chapters.en: And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it most holy unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你要按以色列人被数的，计算总数，你数的时候，他们各人要为自己的生命把赎价奉给耶和华，免得数的时候在他们中间有灾殃。
chapters.en: When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the LORD, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when numberest them.

chapters.zh: 凡过去归那些被数之人的，每人要按圣所的平，拿银子半舍客勒；这半舍客勒是奉给耶和华的礼物（一舍客勒是二十季拉）。
chapters.en: This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel twenty gerahs:) an half shekel the offering of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 凡过去归那些被数的人，从二十岁以外的，要将这礼物奉给耶和华。
chapters.en: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他们为赎生命将礼物奉给耶和华，富足的不可多出，贫穷的也不可少出，各人要出半舍客勒。
chapters.en: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when give an offering unto the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls.

chapters.zh: 你要从以色列人收这赎罪银，作为会幕的使用，可以在耶和华面前为以色列人作纪念，赎生命。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你要用铜做洗濯盆和盆座，以便洗濯。要将盆放在会幕和坛的中间，在盆里盛水。
chapters.en: Thou shalt also make a laver brass, and his foot brass, to wash : and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein.

chapters.zh: 亚伦和他的儿子要在这盆里洗手洗脚。
chapters.en: For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat:

chapters.zh: 他们进会幕，或是就近坛前供职给耶和华献火祭的时候，必用水洗濯，免得死亡。
chapters.en: When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the LORD:

chapters.zh: 他们洗手洗脚就免得死亡。这要作亚伦和他后裔世世代代永远的定例。
chapters.en: So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, to him and to his seed throughout their generations.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: Moreover the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你要取上品的香料，就是流质的没药五百舍客勒，香肉桂一半，就是二百五十舍客勒，菖蒲二百五十舍客勒，
chapters.en: Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred , and of sweet cinnamon half so much, two hundred and fifty , and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty ,

chapters.zh: 桂皮五百舍客勒，都按著圣所的平，又取橄榄油一欣，
chapters.en: And of cassia five hundred , after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin:

chapters.zh: 按做香之法调和做成圣膏油。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil.

chapters.zh: 要用这膏油抹会幕和法柜，
chapters.en: And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony,

chapters.zh: 桌子与桌子的一切器具，灯台和灯台的器具，并香坛、
chapters.en: And the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense,

chapters.zh: 燔祭坛，和坛的一切器具，洗濯盆和盆座。
chapters.en: And the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot.

chapters.zh: 要使这些物成为圣，好成为至圣；凡挨著的都成为圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 要膏亚伦和他的儿子，使他们成为圣，可以给我供祭司的职分。
chapters.en: And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that may minister unto me in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 你要对以色列人说：这油，我要世世代代以为圣膏油。
chapters.en: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations.

chapters.zh: 不可倒在别人的身上，也不可按这调和之法做与此相似的。这膏油是圣的，你们也要以为圣。
chapters.en: Upon man's flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make like it, after the composition of it: it holy, it shall be holy unto you.

chapters.zh: 凡调和与此相似的，或将这膏膏在别人身上的，这人要从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Whosoever compoundeth like it, or whosoever putteth of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：你要取馨香的香料，就是拿他弗、施喜列、喜利比拿；这馨香的香料和净乳香各样要一般大的分量。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like :

chapters.zh: 你要用这些加上盐，按做香之法做成清净圣洁的香。
chapters.en: And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure holy:

chapters.zh: 这香要取点捣得极细，放在会幕内、法柜前，我要在那里与你相会。你们要以这香为至圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt beat of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.

chapters.zh: 你们不可按这调和之法为自己做香；要以这香为圣，归耶和华。
chapters.en: And the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD.

chapters.zh: 凡做香和这香一样，为要闻香味的，这人要从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 看哪，犹大支派中，户珥的孙子、乌利的儿子比撒列，我已经题他的名召他。
chapters.en: See, I have called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah:

chapters.zh: 我也以我的灵充满了他，使他有智慧，有聪明，有知识，能做各样的工，
chapters.en: And I have filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, and in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship,

chapters.zh: 能想出巧工，用金、银、铜制造各物，
chapters.en: To devise cunning works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,

chapters.zh: 又能刻宝石，可以镶嵌，能雕刻木头，能做各样的工。
chapters.en: And in cutting of stones, to set , and in carving of timber, to work in all manner of workmanship.

chapters.zh: 我分派但支派中、亚希撒抹的儿子亚何利亚伯与他同工。凡心里有智慧的，我更使他们有智慧，能做我一切所吩咐的，
chapters.en: And I, behold, I have given with him Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan: and in the hearts of all that are wise hearted I have put wisdom, that they may make all that I have commanded thee;

chapters.zh: 就是会幕和法柜，并其上的施恩座，与会幕中一切的器具，
chapters.en: The tabernacle of the congregation, and the ark of the testimony, and the mercy seat that thereupon, and all the furniture of the tabernacle,

chapters.zh: 桌子和桌子的器具，精金的灯台和灯台的一切器具并香坛，
chapters.en: And the table and his furniture, and the pure candlestick with all his furniture, and the altar of incense,

chapters.zh: 燔祭坛和坛的一切器具，并洗濯盆与盆座，
chapters.en: And the altar of burnt offering with all his furniture, and the laver and his foot,

chapters.zh: 精工做的礼服，和祭司亚伦并他儿子用以供祭司职分的圣衣，
chapters.en: And the cloths of service, and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office,

chapters.zh: 膏油和为圣所用馨香的香料。他们都要照我一切所吩咐的去做。
chapters.en: And the anointing oil, and sweet incense for the holy : according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你要吩咐以色列人说：你们务要守我的安息日；因为这是你我之间世世代代的证据，使你们知道我 ─ 耶和华是叫你们成为圣的。
chapters.en: Speak thou also unto the children of Israel, saying, Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that may know that I the LORD that doth sanctify you.

chapters.zh: 所以你们要守安息日，以为圣日。凡干犯这日的，必要把他治死；凡在这日做工的，必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it holy unto you: every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death: for whosoever doeth work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 六日要做工，但第七日是安息圣日，是向耶和华守为圣的。凡在安息日做工的，必要把他治死。
chapters.en: Six days may work be done; but in the seventh the sabbath of rest, holy to the LORD: whosoever doeth work in the sabbath day, he shall surely be put to death.

chapters.zh: 故此，以色列人要世世代代守安息日为永远的约。
chapters.en: Wherefore the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations, a perpetual covenant.

chapters.zh: 这是我和以色列人永远的证据；因为六日之内耶和华造天地，第七日便安息舒畅。
chapters.en: It a sign between me and the children of Israel for ever: for six days the LORD made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested, and was refreshed.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在西乃山和摩西说完了话，就把两块法版交给他，是神用指头写的石版。
chapters.en: And he gave unto Moses, when he had made an end of communing with him upon mount Sinai, two tables of testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger of God.

chapters.zh: 百姓见摩西迟延不下山，就大家聚集到亚伦那里，对他说：起来！为我们做神像，可以在我们前面引路；因为领我们出埃及地的那个摩西，我们不知道他遭了甚么事。
chapters.en: And when the people saw that Moses delayed to come down out of the mount, the people gathered themselves together unto Aaron, and said unto him, Up, make us gods, which shall go before us; for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

chapters.zh: 亚伦对他们说：你们去摘下你们妻子、儿女耳上的金环，拿来给我。
chapters.en: And Aaron said unto them, Break off the golden earrings, which in the ears of your wives, of your sons, and of your daughters, and bring unto me.

chapters.zh: 百姓就都摘下他们耳上的金环，拿来给亚伦。
chapters.en: And all the people brake off the golden earrings which in their ears, and brought unto Aaron.

chapters.zh: 亚伦从他们手里接过来，铸了一只牛犊，用雕刻的器具做成。他们就说：以色列阿，这是领你出埃及地的神。
chapters.en: And he received at their hand, and fashioned it with a graving tool, after he had made it a molten calf: and they said, These thy gods, O Israel, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 亚伦看见，就在牛犊面前筑坛，且宣告说：明日要向耶和华守节。
chapters.en: And when Aaron saw , he built an altar before it; and Aaron made proclamation, and said, To morrow a feast to the LORD.

chapters.zh: 次日清早，百姓起来献燔祭和平安祭，就坐下吃喝，起来玩耍。
chapters.en: And they rose up early on the morrow, and offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings; and the people sat down to eat and to drink, and rose up to play.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：下去罢，因为你的百姓，就是你从埃及地领出来的，已经败坏了。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Go, get thee down; for thy people, which thou broughtest out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted :

chapters.zh: 他们快快偏离了我所吩咐的道，为自己铸了一只牛犊，向他下拜献祭，说：以色列阿，这就是领你出埃及地的神。
chapters.en: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them: they have made them a molten calf, and have worshipped it, and have sacrificed thereunto, and said, These thy gods, O Israel, which have brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：我看这百姓真是硬著颈项的百姓。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, I have seen this people, and, behold, it a stiffnecked people:

chapters.zh: 你且由著我，我要向他们发烈怒，将他们灭绝，使你的后裔成为大国。
chapters.en: Now therefore let me alone, that my wrath may wax hot against them, and that I may consume them: and I will make of thee a great nation.

chapters.zh: 摩西便恳求耶和华 ─ 他的神说：耶和华阿，你为甚么向你的百姓发烈怒呢？这百姓是你用大力和大能的手从埃及地领出来的。
chapters.en: And Moses besought the LORD his God, and said, LORD, why doth thy wrath wax hot against thy people, which thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power, and with a mighty hand?

chapters.zh: 为甚么使埃及人议论说他领他们出去，是要降祸与他们，把他们杀在山中，将他们从地上除灭？求你转意，不发你的烈怒，后悔，不降祸与你的百姓。
chapters.en: Wherefore should the Egyptians speak, and say, For mischief did he bring them out, to slay them in the mountains, and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from thy fierce wrath, and repent of this evil against thy people.

chapters.zh: 求你记念你的仆人亚伯拉罕、以撒、以色列。你曾指著自己起誓说：我必使你们的后裔像天上的星那样多，并且我所应许的这全地，必给你们的后裔，他们要永远承受为业。
chapters.en: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, thy servants, to whom thou swarest by thine own self, and saidst unto them, I will multiply your seed as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken of will I give unto your seed, and they shall inherit for ever.

chapters.zh: 于是耶和华后悔，不把所说的祸降与他的百姓。
chapters.en: And the LORD repented of the evil which he thought to do unto his people.

chapters.zh: 摩西转身下山，手里拿著两块法版。这版是两面写的，这面那面都有字，
chapters.en: And Moses turned, and went down from the mount, and the two tables of the testimony in his hand: the tables written on both their sides; on the one side and on the other they written.

chapters.zh: 是神的工作，字是神写的，刻在版上。
chapters.en: And the tables the work of God, and the writing the writing of God, graven upon the tables.

chapters.zh: 约书亚一听见百姓呼喊的声音，就对摩西说：在营里有争战的声音。
chapters.en: And when Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said unto Moses, a noise of war in the camp.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：这不是人打胜仗的声音，也不是人打败仗的声音，我所听见的乃是人歌唱的声音。
chapters.en: And he said, not the voice of shout for mastery, neither the voice of cry for being overcome: the noise of sing do I hear.

chapters.zh: 摩西挨近营前就看见牛犊，又看见人跳舞，便发烈怒，把两块版扔在山下摔碎了，
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as soon as he came nigh unto the camp, that he saw the calf, and the dancing: and Moses' anger waxed hot, and he cast the tables out of his hands, and brake them beneath the mount.

chapters.zh: 又将他们所铸的牛犊用火焚烧，磨得粉碎，撒在水面上，叫以色列人喝。
chapters.en: And he took the calf which they had made, and burnt in the fire, and ground to powder, and strawed upon the water, and made the children of Israel drink .

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦说：这百姓向你做了甚么？你竟使他们陷在大罪里！
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Aaron, What did this people unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them?

chapters.zh: 亚伦说：求我主不要发烈怒。这百姓专于作恶，是你知道的。
chapters.en: And Aaron said, Let not the anger of my lord wax hot: thou knowest the people, that they on mischief.

chapters.zh: 他们对我说：你为我们做神像，可以在我们前面引路；因为领我们出埃及地的那个摩西，我们不知道他遭了甚么事。
chapters.en: For they said unto me, Make us gods, which shall go before us: for this Moses, the man that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.

chapters.zh: 我对他们说：凡有金环的可以摘下来，他们就给了我。我把金环扔在火中，这牛犊便出来了。
chapters.en: And I said unto them, Whosoever hath any gold, let them break off. So they gave me: then I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.

chapters.zh: 摩西见百姓放肆（亚伦纵容他们，使他们在仇敌中间被讥刺），
chapters.en: And when Moses saw that the people naked; (for Aaron had made them naked unto shame among their enemies:)

chapters.zh: 就站在营门中，说：凡属耶和华的，都要到我这里来！于是利未的子孙都到他那里聚集。
chapters.en: Then Moses stood in the gate of the camp, and said, Who on the LORD'S side? unto me. And all the sons of Levi gathered themselves together unto him.

chapters.zh: 他对他们说：耶和华 ─ 以色列的神这样说：你们各人把刀跨在腰间，在营中往来，从这门到那门，各人杀他的弟兄与同伴并邻舍。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Thus saith the LORD God of Israel, Put every man his sword by his side, go in and out from gate to gate throughout the camp, and slay every man his brother, and every man his companion, and every man his neighbour.

chapters.zh: 利未的子孙照摩西的话行了。那一天百姓中被杀的约有三千。
chapters.en: And the children of Levi did according to the word of Moses: and there fell of the people that day about three thousand men.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：今天你们要自洁，归耶和华为圣，各人攻击他的儿子和弟兄，使耶和华赐福与你们。
chapters.en: For Moses had said, Consecrate yourselves to day to the LORD, even every man upon his son, and upon his brother; that he may bestow upon you a blessing this day.

chapters.zh: 到了第二天，摩西对百姓说：你们犯了大罪。我如今要上耶和华那里去，或者可以为你们赎罪。
chapters.en: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses said unto the people, Ye have sinned a great sin: and now I will go up unto the LORD; peradventure I shall make an atonement for your sin.

chapters.zh: 摩西回到耶和华那里，说：唉！这百姓犯了大罪，为自己做了金像。
chapters.en: And Moses returned unto the LORD, and said, Oh, this people have sinned a great sin, and have made them gods of gold.

chapters.zh: 倘或你肯赦免他们的罪 … … 不然，求你从你所写的册上涂抹我的名。
chapters.en: Yet now, if thou wilt forgive their sin-; and if not, blot me, I pray thee, out of thy book which thou hast written.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：谁得罪我，我就从我的册上涂抹谁的名。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Whosoever hath sinned against me, him will I blot out of my book.

chapters.zh: 现在你去领这百姓，往我所告诉你的地方去，我的使者必在你前面引路；只是到我追讨的日子，我必追讨他们的罪。
chapters.en: Therefore now go, lead the people unto of which I have spoken unto thee: behold, mine Angel shall go before thee: nevertheless in the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华杀百姓的缘故是因他们同亚伦做了牛犊。
chapters.en: And the LORD plagued the people, because they made the calf, which Aaron made.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：我曾起誓应许亚伯拉罕、以撒、雅各说：要将迦南地赐给你的后裔。现在你和你从埃及地所领出来的百姓，要从这里往那地去。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Depart, go up hence, thou and the people which thou hast brought up out of the land of Egypt, unto the land which I sware unto Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, Unto thy seed will I give it:

chapters.zh: 我要差遣使者在你前面，撵出迦南人、亚摩利人、赫人、比利洗人、希未人、耶布斯人，
chapters.en: And I will send an angel before thee; and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:

chapters.zh: 领你到那流奶与蜜之地。我自己不同你们上去；因为你们是硬著颈项的百姓，恐怕我在路上把你们灭绝。
chapters.en: Unto a land flowing with milk and honey: for I will not go up in the midst of thee; for thou a stiffnecked people: lest I consume thee in the way.

chapters.zh: 百姓听见这凶信就悲哀，也没有人佩戴妆饰。
chapters.en: And when the people heard these evil tidings, they mourned: and no man did put on him his ornaments.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你告诉以色列人说：耶和华说：你们是硬著颈项的百姓，我若一霎时临到你们中间，必灭绝你们。现在你们要把身上的妆饰摘下来，使我可以知道怎样待你们
chapters.en: For the LORD had said unto Moses, Say unto the children of Israel, Ye a stiffnecked people: I will come up into the midst of thee in a moment, and consume thee: therefore now put off thy ornaments from thee, that I may know what to do unto thee.

chapters.zh: 以色列人从住何烈山以后，就把身上的妆饰摘得干净。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments by the mount Horeb.

chapters.zh: 摩西素常将帐棚支搭在营外，离营却远，他称这帐棚为会幕。凡求问耶和华的，就到营外的会幕那里去。
chapters.en: And Moses took the tabernacle, and pitched it without the camp, afar off from the camp, and called it the Tabernacle of the congregation. And it came to pass, every one which sought the LORD went out unto the tabernacle of the congregation, which without the camp.

chapters.zh: 当摩西出营到会幕去的时候，百姓就都起来，各人站在自己帐棚的门口，望著摩西，直等到他进了会幕。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when Moses went out unto the tabernacle, all the people rose up, and stood every man his tent door, and looked after Moses, until he was gone into the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 摩西进会幕的时候，云柱降下来，立在会幕的门前，耶和华便与摩西说话。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as Moses entered into the tabernacle, the cloudy pillar descended, and stood the door of the tabernacle, and talked with Moses.

chapters.zh: 众百姓看见云柱立在会幕门前，就都起来，各人在自己帐棚的门口下拜。
chapters.en: And all the people saw the cloudy pillar stand the tabernacle door: and all the people rose up and worshipped, every man his tent door.

chapters.zh: 耶和华与摩西面对面说话，好像人与朋友说话一般。摩西转到营里去，惟有他的帮手 ─ 一个少年人嫩的儿子约书亚不离开会幕。
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend. And he turned again into the camp: but his servant Joshua, the son of Nun, a young man, departed not out of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 摩西对耶和华说：你吩咐我说：将这百姓领上去，却没有叫我知道你要打发谁与我同去，只说：我按你的名认识你，你在我眼前也蒙了恩。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the LORD, See, thou sayest unto me, Bring up this people: and thou hast not let me know whom thou wilt send with me. Yet thou hast said, I know thee by name, and thou hast also found grace in my sight.

chapters.zh: 我如今若在你眼前蒙恩，求你将你的道指示我，使我可以认识你，好在你眼前蒙恩。求你想到这民是你的民。
chapters.en: Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace in thy sight, shew me now thy way, that I may know thee, that I may find grace in thy sight: and consider that this nation thy people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我必亲自和你同去，使你得安息。
chapters.en: And he said, My presence shall go , and I will give thee rest.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：你若不亲自和我同去，就不要把我们从这里领上去。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, If thy presence go not , carry us not up hence.

chapters.zh: 人在何事上得以知道我和你的百姓在你眼前蒙恩呢？岂不是因你与我们同去、使我和你的百姓与地上的万民有分别么？
chapters.en: For wherein shall it be known here that I and thy people have found grace in thy sight? not in that thou goest with us? so shall we be separated, I and thy people, from all the people that upon the face of the earth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你这所求的我也要行；因为你在我眼前蒙了恩，并且我按你的名认识你。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, I will do this thing also that thou hast spoken: for thou hast found grace in my sight, and I know thee by name.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：求你显出你的荣耀给我看。
chapters.en: And he said, I beseech thee, shew me thy glory.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我要显我一切的恩慈，在你面前经过，宣告我的名。我要恩待谁就恩待谁；要怜悯谁就怜悯谁；
chapters.en: And he said, I will make all my goodness pass before thee, and I will proclaim the name of the LORD before thee; and will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will shew mercy on whom I will shew mercy.

chapters.zh: 又说：你不能看见我的面，因为人见我的面不能存活。
chapters.en: And he said, Thou canst not see my face: for there shall no man see me, and live.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：看哪，在我这里有地方，你要站在磐石上。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, Behold, a place by me, and thou shalt stand upon a rock:

chapters.zh: 我的荣耀经过的时候，我必将你放在磐石穴中，用我的手遮掩你，等我过去，
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, while my glory passeth by, that I will put thee in a clift of the rock, and will cover thee with my hand while I pass by:

chapters.zh: 然后我要将我的手收回，你就得见我的背，却不得见我的面。
chapters.en: And I will take away mine hand, and thou shalt see my back parts: but my face shall not be seen.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：你要凿出两块石版，和先前你摔碎的那版一样；其上的字我要写在这版上。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Hew thee two tables of stone like unto the first: and I will write upon tables the words that were in the first tables, which thou brakest.

chapters.zh: 明日早晨，你要预备好了，上西乃山，在山顶上站在我面前。
chapters.en: And be ready in the morning, and come up in the morning unto mount Sinai, and present thyself there to me in the top of the mount.

chapters.zh: 谁也不可和你一同上去，遍山都不可有人，在山根也不可叫羊群牛群吃草。
chapters.en: And no man shall come up with thee, neither let any man be seen throughout all the mount; neither let the flocks nor herds feed before that mount.

chapters.zh: 摩西就凿出两块石版，和先前的一样。清晨起来，照耶和华所吩咐的上西乃山去，手里拿著两块石版。
chapters.en: And he hewed two tables of stone like unto the first; and Moses rose up early in the morning, and went up unto mount Sinai, as the LORD had commanded him, and took in his hand the two tables of stone.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在云中降临，和摩西一同站在那里，宣告耶和华的名。
chapters.en: And the LORD descended in the cloud, and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在他面前宣告说：耶和华，耶和华，是有怜悯有恩典的神，不轻易发怒，并有丰盛的慈爱和诚实，
chapters.en: And the LORD passed by before him, and proclaimed, The LORD, The LORD God, merciful and gracious, longsuffering, and abundant in goodness and truth,

chapters.zh: 为千万人存留慈爱，赦免罪孽、过犯，和罪恶，万不以有罪的为无罪，必追讨他的罪，自父及子，直到三、四代。
chapters.en: Keeping mercy for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, and that will by no means clear ; visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, and upon the children's children, unto the third and to the fourth .

chapters.zh: 摩西急忙伏地下拜，
chapters.en: And Moses made haste, and bowed his head toward the earth, and worshipped.

chapters.zh: 说：主阿，我若在你眼前蒙恩，求你在我们中间同行，因为这是硬著颈项的百姓。又求你赦免我们的罪孽和罪恶，以我们为你的产业。
chapters.en: And he said, If now I have found grace in thy sight, O Lord, let my Lord, I pray thee, go among us; for it a stiffnecked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for thine inheritance.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我要立约，要在百姓面前行奇妙的事，是在遍地万国中所未曾行的。在你四围的外邦人就要看见耶和华的作为，因我向你所行的是可畏惧的事。
chapters.en: And he said, Behold, I make a covenant: before all thy people I will do marvels, such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation: and all the people among which thou shall see the work of the LORD: for it a terrible thing that I will do with thee.

chapters.zh: 我今天所吩咐你的，你要谨守。我要从你面前撵出亚摩利人、迦南人、赫人、比利洗人、希未人、耶布斯人。
chapters.en: Observe thou that which I command thee this day: behold, I drive out before thee the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

chapters.zh: 你要谨慎，不可与你所去那地的居民立约，恐怕成为你们中间的网罗；
chapters.en: Take heed to thyself, lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land whither thou goest, lest it be for a snare in the midst of thee:

chapters.zh: 却要拆毁他们的祭坛，打碎他们的柱像，砍下他们的木偶。
chapters.en: But ye shall destroy their altars, break their images, and cut down their groves:

chapters.zh: 不可敬拜别神；因为耶和华是忌邪的神，名为忌邪者。
chapters.en: For thou shalt worship no other god: for the LORD, whose name Jealous, a jealous God:

chapters.zh: 只怕你与那地的居民立约，百姓随从他们的神，就行邪淫，祭祀他们的神，有人叫你，你便吃他的祭物，
chapters.en: Lest thou make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and they go a whoring after their gods, and do sacrifice unto their gods, and call thee, and thou eat of his sacrifice;

chapters.zh: 又为你的儿子娶他们的女儿为妻，他们的女儿随从他们的神，就行邪淫，使你的儿子也随从他们的神行邪淫。
chapters.en: And thou take of their daughters unto thy sons, and their daughters go a whoring after their gods, and make thy sons go a whoring after their gods.

chapters.zh: 不可为自己铸造神像。
chapters.en: Thou shalt make thee no molten gods.

chapters.zh: 你要守除酵节，照我所吩咐你的，在亚笔月内所定的日期吃无酵饼七天，因为你是这亚笔月内出了埃及。
chapters.en: The feast of unleavened bread shalt thou keep. Seven days thou shalt eat unleavened bread, as I commanded thee, in the time of the month Abib: for in the month Abib thou camest out from Egypt.

chapters.zh: 凡头生的都是我的；一切牲畜头生的，无论是牛是羊，公的都是我的。
chapters.en: All that openeth the matrix mine; and every firstling among thy cattle, ox or sheep, .

chapters.zh: 头生的驴要用羊羔代赎，若不代赎就要打折他的颈项。凡头生的儿子都要赎出来。谁也不可空手朝见我。
chapters.en: But the firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a lamb: and if thou redeem not, then shalt thou break his neck. All the firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem. And none shall appear before me empty.

chapters.zh: 你六日要做工，第七日要安息，虽在耕种收割的时候也要安息。
chapters.en: Six days thou shalt work, but on the seventh day thou shalt rest: in earing time and in harvest thou shalt rest.

chapters.zh: 在收割初熟麦子的时候要守七七节；又在年底要守收藏节。
chapters.en: And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the year's end.

chapters.zh: 你们一切男丁要一年三次朝见主耶和华 ─ 以色列的神。
chapters.en: Thrice in the year shall all your men children appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我要从你面前赶出外邦人，扩张你的境界。你一年三次上去朝见耶和华 ─ 你神的时候，必没有人贪慕你的地土。
chapters.en: For I will cast out the nations before thee, and enlarge thy borders: neither shall any man desire thy land, when thou shalt go up to appear before the LORD thy God thrice in the year.

chapters.zh: 你不可将我祭物的血和有酵的饼一同献上。逾越节的祭物也不可留到早晨。
chapters.en: Thou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leaven; neither shall the sacrifice of the feast of the passover be left unto the morning.

chapters.zh: 地里首先初熟之物要送到耶和华 ─ 你神的殿。不可用山羊羔母的奶煮山羊羔。
chapters.en: The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring unto the house of the LORD thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：你要将这些话写上，因为我是按这话与你和以色列人立约。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Write thou these words: for after the tenor of these words I have made a covenant with thee and with Israel.

chapters.zh: 摩西在耶和华那里四十昼夜，也不吃饭也不喝水。耶和华将这约的话，就是十条诫，写在两块版上。
chapters.en: And he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did neither eat bread, nor drink water. And he wrote upon the tables the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.

chapters.zh: 摩西手里拿著两块法版下西乃山的时候，不知道自己的面皮因耶和华和他说话就发了光。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when Moses came down from mount Sinai with the two tables of testimony in Moses' hand, when he came down from the mount, that Moses wist not that the skin of his face shone while he talked with him.

chapters.zh: 亚伦和以色列众人看见摩西的面皮发光就怕挨近他。
chapters.en: And when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone; and they were afraid to come nigh him.

chapters.zh: 摩西叫他们来；于是亚伦和会众的官长都到他那里去，摩西就与他们说话。
chapters.en: And Moses called unto them; and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned unto him: and Moses talked with them.

chapters.zh: 随后以色列众人都近前来，他就把耶和华在西乃山与他所说的一切话都吩咐他们。
chapters.en: And afterward all the children of Israel came nigh: and he gave them in commandment all that the LORD had spoken with him in mount Sinai.

chapters.zh: 摩西与他们说完了话就用帕子蒙上脸。
chapters.en: And Moses had done speaking with them, he put a vail on his face.

chapters.zh: 但摩西进到耶和华面前与他说话就揭去帕子，及至出来的时候便将耶和华所吩咐的告诉以色列人。
chapters.en: But when Moses went in before the LORD to speak with him, he took the vail off, until he came out. And he came out, and spake unto the children of Israel which he was commanded.

chapters.zh: 以色列人看见摩西的面皮发光。摩西又用帕子蒙上脸，等到他进去与耶和华说话就揭去帕子。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses' face shone: and Moses put the vail upon his face again, until he went in to speak with him.

chapters.zh: 摩西招聚以色列全会众，对他们说：这是耶和华所吩咐的话，叫你们照著行：
chapters.en: And Moses gathered all the congregation of the children of Israel together, and said unto them, These the words which the LORD hath commanded, that should do them.

chapters.zh: 六日要做工，第七日乃为圣日，当向耶和华守为安息圣日。凡这日之内做工的，必把他治死。
chapters.en: Six days shall work be done, but on the seventh day there shall be to you an holy day, a sabbath of rest to the LORD: whosoever doeth work therein shall be put to death.

chapters.zh: 当安息日，不可在你们一切的住处生火。
chapters.en: Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day.

chapters.zh: 摩西对以色列全会众说：耶和华所吩咐的是这样：
chapters.en: And Moses spake unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, This the thing which the LORD commanded, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们中间要拿礼物献给耶和华，凡乐意献的可以拿耶和华的礼物来，就是金、银、铜，
chapters.en: Take ye from among you an offering unto the LORD: whosoever of a willing heart, let him bring it, an offering of the LORD; gold, and silver, and brass,

chapters.zh: 蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，细麻，山羊毛，
chapters.en: And blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' ,

chapters.zh: 染红的公羊皮，海狗皮，皂荚木，
chapters.en: And rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood,

chapters.zh: 点灯的油，并做膏油和香的香料，
chapters.en: And oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense,

chapters.zh: 红玛瑙与别样的宝石，可以镶嵌在以弗得和胸牌上
chapters.en: And onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod, and for the breastplate.

chapters.zh: 你们中间凡心里有智慧的都要来做耶和华一切所吩咐的：
chapters.en: And every wise hearted among you shall come, and make all that the LORD hath commanded;

chapters.zh: 就是帐幕和帐幕的罩棚，并帐幕的盖、钩子、板、闩、柱子、带卯的座，
chapters.en: The tabernacle, his tent, and his covering, his taches, and his boards, his bars, his pillars, and his sockets,

chapters.zh: 柜和柜的杠，施恩座和遮掩柜的幔子，
chapters.en: The ark, and the staves thereof, the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering,

chapters.zh: 桌子和桌子的杠与桌子的一切器具，并陈设饼，
chapters.en: The table, and his staves, and all his vessels, and the shewbread,

chapters.zh: 灯台和灯台的器具，灯盏并点灯的油，
chapters.en: The candlestick also for the light, and his furniture, and his lamps, with the oil for the light,

chapters.zh: 香坛和坛的杠，膏油和馨香的香料，并帐幕门口的帘子，
chapters.en: And the incense altar, and his staves, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the door at the entering in of the tabernacle,

chapters.zh: 燔祭坛和坛的铜网，坛的杠并坛的一切器具，洗濯盆和盆座，
chapters.en: The altar of burnt offering, with his brasen grate, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot,

chapters.zh: 院子的帷子和帷子的柱子，带卯的座和院子的门帘，
chapters.en: The hangings of the court, his pillars, and their sockets, and the hanging for the door of the court,

chapters.zh: 帐幕的橛子并院子的橛子，和这两处的绳子，
chapters.en: The pins of the tabernacle, and the pins of the court, and their cords,

chapters.zh: 精工做的礼服和祭司亚伦并他儿子在圣所用以供祭司职分的圣衣。
chapters.en: The cloths of service, to do service in the holy , the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众从摩西面前退去。
chapters.en: And all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses.

chapters.zh: 凡心里受感和甘心乐意的都拿耶和华的礼物来，用以做会幕和其中一切的使用，又用以做圣衣。
chapters.en: And they came, every one whose heart stirred him up, and every one whom his spirit made willing, they brought the LORD'S offering to the work of the tabernacle of the congregation, and for all his service, and for the holy garments.

chapters.zh: 凡心里乐意献礼物的，连男带女，各将金器，就是胸前针、耳环（或作：鼻环）、打印的戒指，和手钏带来献给耶和华。
chapters.en: And they came, both men and women, as many as were willing hearted, brought bracelets, and earrings, and rings, and tablets, all jewels of gold: and every man that offered an offering of gold unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 凡有蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，细麻，山羊毛，染红的公羊皮，海狗皮的，都拿了来；
chapters.en: And every man, with whom was found blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' , and red skins of rams, and badgers' skins, brought .

chapters.zh: 凡献银子和铜给耶和华为礼物的都拿了来；凡有皂荚木可做甚么使用的也拿了来。
chapters.en: Every one that did offer an offering of silver and brass brought the LORD'S offering: and every man, with whom was found shittim wood for any work of the service, brought .

chapters.zh: 凡心中有智慧的妇女亲手纺线，把所纺的蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和细麻都拿了来。
chapters.en: And all the women that were wise hearted did spin with their hands, and brought that which they had spun, of blue, and of purple, of scarlet, and of fine linen.

chapters.zh: 凡有智慧、心里受感的妇女就纺山羊毛。
chapters.en: And all the women whose heart stirred them up in wisdom spun goats' .

chapters.zh: 众官长把红玛瑙和别样的宝石，可以镶嵌在以弗得与胸牌上的，都拿了来；
chapters.en: And the rulers brought onyx stones, and stones to be set, for the ephod, and for the breastplate;

chapters.zh: 又拿香料做香，拿油点灯，做膏油。
chapters.en: And spice, and oil for the light, and for the anointing oil, and for the sweet incense.

chapters.zh: 以色列人，无论男女，凡甘心乐意献礼物给耶和华的，都将礼物拿来，做耶和华藉摩西所吩咐的一切工。
chapters.en: The children of Israel brought a willing offering unto the LORD, every man and woman, whose heart made them willing to bring for all manner of work, which the LORD had commanded to be made by the hand of Moses.

chapters.zh: 摩西对以色列人说：犹大支派中，户珥的孙子、乌利的儿子比撒列，耶和华已经题他的名召他，
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the children of Israel, See, the LORD hath called by name Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah;

chapters.zh: 又以神的灵充满了他，使他有智慧、聪明、知识，能做各样的工，
chapters.en: And he hath filled him with the spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, and in knowledge, and in all manner of workmanship;

chapters.zh: 能想出巧工，用金、银、铜制造各物，
chapters.en: And to devise curious works, to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass,

chapters.zh: 又能刻宝石，可以镶嵌，能雕刻木头，能做各样的巧工。
chapters.en: And in the cutting of stones, to set , and in carving of wood, to make any manner of cunning work.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又使他，和但支派中亚希撒抹的儿子亚何利亚伯，心里灵明，能教导人。
chapters.en: And he hath put in his heart that he may teach, he, and Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.

chapters.zh: 耶和华使他们的心满有智慧，能做各样的工，无论是雕刻的工，巧匠的工，用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和细麻、绣花的工，并机匠的工，他们都能做，也能想出奇巧的工。
chapters.en: Them hath he filled with wisdom of heart, to work all manner of work, of the engraver, and of the cunning workman, and of the embroiderer, in blue, and in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen, and of the weaver, of them that do any work, and of those that devise cunning work.

chapters.zh: 比撒列和亚何利亚伯，并一切心里有智慧的，就是蒙耶和华赐智慧聪明、叫他知道做圣所各样使用之工的，都要照耶和华所吩咐的做工。
chapters.en: Then wrought Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whom the LORD put wisdom and understanding to know how to work all manner of work for the service of the sanctuary, according to all that the LORD had commanded.

chapters.zh: 凡耶和华赐他心里有智慧、而且受感前来做这工的，摩西把他们和比撒列并亚何利亚伯一同召来。
chapters.en: And Moses called Bezaleel and Aholiab, and every wise hearted man, in whose heart the LORD had put wisdom, every one whose heart stirred him up to come unto the work to do it:

chapters.zh: 这些人就从摩西收了以色列人为做圣所并圣所使用之工所拿来的礼物。百姓每早晨还把甘心献的礼物拿来。
chapters.en: And they received of Moses all the offering, which the children of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary, to make it . And they brought yet unto him free offerings every morning.

chapters.zh: 凡做圣所一切工的智慧人各都离开他所作的工，
chapters.en: And all the wise men, that wrought all the work of the sanctuary, came every man from his work which they made;

chapters.zh: 来对摩西说：百姓为耶和华吩咐使用之工所拿来的，富富有余。
chapters.en: And they spake unto Moses, saying, The people bring much more than enough for the service of the work, which the LORD commanded to make.

chapters.zh: 摩西传命，他们就在全营中宣告说：无论男女，不必再为圣所拿甚么礼物来。这样才拦住百姓不再拿礼物来。
chapters.en: And Moses gave commandment, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, Let neither man nor woman make any more work for the offering of the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing.

chapters.zh: 因为他们所有的材料够做一切当做的物，而且有余。
chapters.en: For the stuff they had was sufficient for all the work to make it, and too much.

chapters.zh: 他们中间，凡心里有智慧做工的，用十幅幔子做帐幕。这幔子是比撒列用撚的细麻和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线制造的，并用巧匠的手工绣上基路伯。
chapters.en: And every wise hearted man among them that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: cherubims of cunning work made he them.

chapters.zh: 每幅幔子长二十八肘，宽四肘，都是一样的尺寸。
chapters.en: The length of one curtain twenty and eight cubits, and the breadth of one curtain four cubits: the curtains all of one size.

chapters.zh: 他使这五幅幔子幅幅相连，又使那五幅幔子幅幅相连；
chapters.en: And he coupled the five curtains one unto another: and five curtains he coupled one unto another.

chapters.zh: 在这相连的幔子末幅边上做蓝色的钮扣，在那相连的幔子末幅边上也照样做；
chapters.en: And he made loops of blue on the edge of one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling: likewise he made in the uttermost side of curtain, in the coupling of the second.

chapters.zh: 在这相连的幔子上做五十个钮扣，在那相连的幔子上也做五十个钮扣，都是两两相对；
chapters.en: Fifty loops made he in one curtain, and fifty loops made he in the edge of the curtain which in the coupling of the second: the loops held one to another.

chapters.zh: 又做五十个金钩，使幔子相连。这才成了一个帐幕。
chapters.en: And he made fifty taches of gold, and coupled the curtains one unto another with the taches: so it became one tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 他用山羊毛织十一幅幔子，作为帐幕以上的罩棚。
chapters.en: And he made curtains goats' for the tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains he made them.

chapters.zh: 每幅幔子长三十肘，宽四肘；十一幅幔子都是一样的尺寸。
chapters.en: The length of one curtain thirty cubits, and four cubits the breadth of one curtain: the eleven curtains of one size.

chapters.zh: 他把五幅幔子连成一幅，又把六幅幔子连成一幅；
chapters.en: And he coupled five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves.

chapters.zh: 在这相连的幔子末幅边上做五十个钮扣，在那相连的幔子末幅边上也做五十个钮扣；
chapters.en: And he made fifty loops upon the uttermost edge of the curtain in the coupling, and fifty loops made he upon the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second.

chapters.zh: 又做五十个铜钩，使罩棚连成一个；
chapters.en: And he made fifty taches brass to couple the tent together, that it might be one.

chapters.zh: 并用染红的公羊皮做罩棚的盖，再用海狗皮做一层罩棚上的顶盖。
chapters.en: And he made a covering for the tent rams' skins dyed red, and a covering badgers' skins above .

chapters.zh: 他用皂荚木做帐幕的竖板。
chapters.en: And he made boards for the tabernacle shittim wood, standing up.

chapters.zh: 每块长十肘，宽一肘半；
chapters.en: The length of a board ten cubits, and the breadth of a board one cubit and a half.

chapters.zh: 每块有两榫相对。帐幕一切的板都是这样做。
chapters.en: One board had two tenons, equally distant one from another: thus did he make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 帐幕的南面做板二十块。
chapters.en: And he made boards for the tabernacle; twenty boards for the south side southward:

chapters.zh: 在这二十块板底下又做四十个带卯的银座：两卯接这块板上的两榫，两卯接那块板上的两榫。
chapters.en: And forty sockets of silver he made under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for his two tenons, and two sockets under another board for his two tenons.

chapters.zh: 帐幕的第二面，就是北面，也做板二十块
chapters.en: And for the other side of the tabernacle, toward the north corner, he made twenty boards,

chapters.zh: 和带卯的银座四十个：这板底下有两卯，那板底下也有两卯。
chapters.en: And their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.

chapters.zh: 帐幕的后面，就是西面，做板六块。
chapters.en: And for the sides of the tabernacle westward he made six boards.

chapters.zh: 帐幕后面的拐角做板两块。
chapters.en: And two boards made he for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides.

chapters.zh: 板的下半截是双的，上半截是整的，直到第一个环子；在帐幕的两个拐角上都是这样做。
chapters.en: And they were coupled beneath, and coupled together at the head thereof, to one ring: thus he did to both of them in both the corners.

chapters.zh: 有八块板和十六个带卯的银座，每块板底下有两卯。
chapters.en: And there were eight boards; and their sockets sixteen sockets of silver, under every board two sockets.

chapters.zh: 他用皂荚木做闩：为帐幕这面的板做五闩，
chapters.en: And he made bars of shittim wood; five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,

chapters.zh: 为帐幕那面的板做五闩，又为帐幕后面的板做五闩，
chapters.en: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the sides westward.

chapters.zh: 使板腰间的中闩从这一头通到那一头。
chapters.en: And he made the middle bar to shoot through the boards from the one end to the other.

chapters.zh: 用金子将板包裹，又做板上的金环套闩；闩也用金子包裹。
chapters.en: And he overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings gold places for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.

chapters.zh: 他用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和撚的细麻织幔子，以巧匠的手工绣上基路伯。
chapters.en: And he made a vail blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: cherubims made he it of cunning work.

chapters.zh: 为幔子做四根皂荚木柱子，用金包裹，柱子上有金钩，又为柱子铸了四个带卯的银座。
chapters.en: And he made thereunto four pillars shittim , and overlaid them with gold: their hooks gold; and he cast for them four sockets of silver.

chapters.zh: 拿蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和撚的细麻，用绣花的手工织帐幕的门帘；
chapters.en: And he made an hanging for the tabernacle door blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, of needlework;

chapters.zh: 又为帘子做五根柱子和柱子上的钩子，用金子把柱顶和柱子上的杆子包裹。柱子有五个带卯的座，是铜的。
chapters.en: And the five pillars of it with their hooks: and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold: but their five sockets brass.

chapters.zh: 比撒列用皂荚木做柜，长二肘半，宽一肘半，高一肘半。
chapters.en: And Bezaleel made the ark shittim wood: two cubits and a half the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it:

chapters.zh: 里外包上精金，四围镶上金牙边，
chapters.en: And he overlaid it with pure gold within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about.

chapters.zh: 又铸四个金环，安在柜的四脚上：这边两环，那边两环。
chapters.en: And he cast for it four rings of gold, by the four corners of it; even two rings upon the one side of it, and two rings upon the other side of it.

chapters.zh: 用皂荚木做两根杠，用金包裹。
chapters.en: And he made staves shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.

chapters.zh: 把杠穿在柜旁的环内，以便抬柜。
chapters.en: And he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, to bear the ark.

chapters.zh: 用精金做施恩座，长二肘半，宽一肘半。
chapters.en: And he made the mercy seat pure gold: two cubits and a half the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof.

chapters.zh: 用金子锤出两个基路伯来，安在施恩座的两头，
chapters.en: And he made two cherubims gold, beaten out of one piece made he them, on the two ends of the mercy seat;

chapters.zh: 这头做一个基路伯，那头做一个基路伯，二基路伯接连一块，在施恩座的两头。
chapters.en: One cherub on the end on this side, and another cherub on the end on that side: out of the mercy seat made he the cherubims on the two ends thereof.

chapters.zh: 二基路伯高张翅膀，遮掩施恩座；基路伯是脸对脸，朝著施恩座。
chapters.en: And the cherubims spread out wings on high, covered with their wings over the mercy seat, with their faces one to another; to the mercy seatward were the faces of the cherubims.

chapters.zh: 他用皂荚木做一张桌子，长二肘，宽一肘，高一肘半，
chapters.en: And he made the table shittim wood: two cubits the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof:

chapters.zh: 又包上精金，四围镶上金牙边。
chapters.en: And he overlaid it with pure gold, and made thereunto a crown of gold round about.

chapters.zh: 桌子的四围各做一掌宽的横梁，横梁上镶著金牙边，
chapters.en: Also he made thereunto a border of an handbreadth round about; and made a crown of gold for the border thereof round about.

chapters.zh: 又铸了四个金环，安在桌子四脚的四角上。
chapters.en: And he cast for it four rings of gold, and put the rings upon the four corners that in the four feet thereof.

chapters.zh: 安环子的地方是挨近横梁，可以穿杠抬桌子。
chapters.en: Over against the border were the rings, the places for the staves to bear the table.

chapters.zh: 他用皂荚木做两根杠，用金包裹，以便抬桌子；
chapters.en: And he made the staves shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold, to bear the table.

chapters.zh: 又用精金做桌子上的器皿，就是盘子、调羹，并奠酒的瓶和爵。
chapters.en: And he made the vessels which upon the table, his dishes, and his spoons, and his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, pure gold.

chapters.zh: 他用精金做一个灯台；这灯台的座和干，与杯、球、花，都是接连一块锤出来的。
chapters.en: And he made the candlestick pure gold: beaten work made he the candlestick; his shaft, and his branch, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, were of the same:

chapters.zh: 灯台两旁杈出六个枝子：这旁三个，那旁三个。
chapters.en: And six branches going out of the sides thereof; three branches of the candlestick out of the one side thereof, and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side thereof:

chapters.zh: 这旁每枝上有三个杯，形状像杏花，有球有花；那旁每枝上也有三个杯，形状像杏花，有球有花。从灯台杈出来的六个枝子都是如此。
chapters.en: Three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch, a knop and a flower; and three bowls made like almonds in another branch, a knop and a flower: so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick.

chapters.zh: 灯台上有四个杯，形状像杏花，有球有花。
chapters.en: And in the candlestick four bowls made like almonds, his knops, and his flowers:

chapters.zh: 灯台每两个枝子以下有球，与枝子接连一块；灯台杈出的六个枝子都是如此。
chapters.en: And a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, and a knop under two branches of the same, according to the six branches going out of it.

chapters.zh: 球和枝子是接连一块，都是一块精金锤出来的。
chapters.en: Their knops and their branches were of the same: all of it one beaten work pure gold.

chapters.zh: 用精金做灯台的七个灯盏，并灯台的蜡剪和蜡花盘。
chapters.en: And he made his seven lamps, and his snuffers, and his snuffdishes, pure gold.

chapters.zh: 他用精金一他连得做灯台和灯台的一切器具。
chapters.en: a talent of pure gold made he it, and all the vessels thereof.

chapters.zh: 他用皂荚木做香坛，是四方的，长一肘，宽一肘，高二肘，坛的四角与坛接连一块；
chapters.en: And he made the incense altar shittim wood: the length of it a cubit, and the breadth of it a cubit; foursquare; and two cubits the height of it; the horns thereof were of the same.

chapters.zh: 又用精金把坛的上面与坛的四面并坛的四角包裹，又在坛的四围镶上金牙边。
chapters.en: And he overlaid it with pure gold, the top of it, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns of it: also he made unto it a crown of gold round about.

chapters.zh: 做两个金环，安在牙子边以下，在坛的两旁、两根横撑上，作为穿杠的用处，以便抬坛。
chapters.en: And he made two rings of gold for it under the crown thereof, by the two corners of it, upon the two sides thereof, to be places for the staves to bear it withal.

chapters.zh: 用皂荚木做杠，用金包裹。
chapters.en: And he made the staves shittim wood, and overlaid them with gold.

chapters.zh: 又按做香之法做圣膏油和馨香料的净香。
chapters.en: And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of sweet spices, according to the work of the apothecary.

chapters.zh: 他用皂荚木做燔祭坛，是四方的，长五肘，宽五肘，高三肘，
chapters.en: And he made the altar of burnt offering shittim wood: five cubits the length thereof, and five cubits the breadth thereof; foursquare; and three cubits the height thereof.

chapters.zh: 在坛的四拐角上做四个角，与坛接连一块，用铜把坛包裹。
chapters.en: And he made the horns thereof on the four corners of it; the horns thereof were of the same: and he overlaid it with brass.

chapters.zh: 他做坛上的盆、铲子、盘子、肉锸子、火鼎；这一切器具都是用铜做的。
chapters.en: And he made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, and the shovels, and the basons, the fleshhooks, and the firepans: all the vessels thereof made he brass.

chapters.zh: 又为坛做一个铜网，安在坛四面的围腰板以下，从下达到坛的半腰。
chapters.en: And he made for the altar a brasen grate of network under the compass thereof beneath unto the midst of it.

chapters.zh: 为铜网的四角铸四个环子，作为穿杠的用处。
chapters.en: And he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass, places for the staves.

chapters.zh: 用皂荚木做杠，用铜包裹，
chapters.en: And he made the staves shittim wood, and overlaid them with brass.

chapters.zh: 把杠穿在坛两旁的环子内，用以抬坛，并用板做坛；坛是空的。
chapters.en: And he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the altar, to bear it withal; he made the altar hollow with boards.

chapters.zh: 他用铜做洗濯盆和盆座，是用会幕门前伺候的妇人之镜子作的。
chapters.en: And he made the laver brass, and the foot of it brass, of the lookingglasses of assembling, which assembled the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 他做帐幕的院子。院子的南面用撚的细麻做帷子，宽一百肘。
chapters.en: And he made the court: on the south side southward the hangings of the court fine twined linen, an hundred cubits:

chapters.zh: 帷子的柱子二十根，带卯的铜座二十个；柱子上的钩子和杆子都是用银子做的。
chapters.en: Their pillars twenty, and their brasen sockets twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

chapters.zh: 北面也有帷子，宽一百肘。帷子的柱子二十根，带卯的铜座二十个；柱子上的钩子和杆子都是用银子做的。
chapters.en: And for the north side an hundred cubits, their pillars twenty, and their sockets of brass twenty; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

chapters.zh: 院子的西面有帷子，宽五十肘。帷子的柱子十根，带卯的座十个；柱子的钩子和杆子都是用银子做的。
chapters.en: And for the west side hangings of fifty cubits, their pillars ten, and their sockets ten; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

chapters.zh: 院子的东面，宽五十肘。
chapters.en: And for the east side eastward fifty cubits.

chapters.zh: 门这边的帷子十五肘，那边也是一样。帷子的柱子三根，带卯的座三个。在门的左右各有帷子十五肘，帷子的柱子三根，带卯的座三个。
chapters.en: The hangings of the one side fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: And for the other side of the court gate, on this hand and that hand, hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

chapters.zh: 院子四面的帷子都是用撚的细麻做的。
chapters.en: All the hangings of the court round about of fine twined linen.

chapters.zh: 柱子带卯的座是铜的，柱子上的钩子和杆子是银的，柱顶是用银子包的。院子一切的柱子都是用银杆连络的。
chapters.en: And the sockets for the pillars brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver; and the overlaying of their chapiters silver; and all the pillars of the court filleted with silver.

chapters.zh: 院子的门帘是以绣花的手工，用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和撚的细麻织的，宽二十肘，高五肘，与院子的帷子相配。
chapters.en: And the hanging for the gate of the court needlework, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: and twenty cubits the length, and the height in the breadth five cubits, answerable to the hangings of the court.

chapters.zh: 帷子的柱子四根，带卯的铜座四个；柱子上的钩子和杆子是银的；柱顶是用银子包的。
chapters.en: And their pillars four, and their sockets brass four; their hooks silver, and the overlaying of their chapiters and their fillets silver.

chapters.zh: 帐幕一切的橛子和院子四围的橛子都是铜的。
chapters.en: And all the pins of the tabernacle, and of the court round about, brass.

chapters.zh: 这是法柜的帐幕中利未人所用物件的总数，是照摩西的吩咐，经祭司亚伦的儿子以他玛的手数点的。
chapters.en: This is the sum of the tabernacle, of the tabernacle of testimony, as it was counted, according to the commandment of Moses, the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, son to Aaron the priest.

chapters.zh: 凡耶和华所吩咐摩西的都是犹大支派户珥的孙子、乌利的儿子比撒列做的。
chapters.en: And Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 与他同工的有但支派中亚希撒抹的儿子亚何利亚伯；他是雕刻匠，又是巧匠，又能用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，和细麻绣花。
chapters.en: And with him Aholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a cunning workman, and an embroiderer in blue, and in purple, and in scarlet, and fine linen.

chapters.zh: 为圣所一切工作使用所献的金子，按圣所的平，有二十九他连得并七百三十舍客勒。
chapters.en: All the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy , even the gold of the offering, was twenty and nine talents, and seven hundred and thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 会中被数的人所出的银子，按圣所的平，有一百他连得并一千七百七十五舍客勒。
chapters.en: And the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation an hundred talents, and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:

chapters.zh: 凡过去归那些被数之人的，从二十岁以外，有六十万零三千五百五十人。按圣所的平，每人出银半舍客勒，就是一比加。
chapters.en: A bekah for every man, , half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for every one that went to be numbered, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty .

chapters.zh: 用那一百他连得银子铸造圣所带卯的座和幔子柱子带卯的座；一百他连得共一百带卯的座，每带卯的座用一他连得。
chapters.en: And of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the vail; an hundred sockets of the hundred talents, a talent for a socket.

chapters.zh: 用那一千七百七十五舍客勒银子做柱子上的钩子，包裹柱顶并柱子上的杆子。
chapters.en: And of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, and overlaid their chapiters, and filleted them.

chapters.zh: 所献的铜有七十他连得并二千四百舍客勒。
chapters.en: And the brass of the offering seventy talents, and two thousand and four hundred shekels.

chapters.zh: 用这铜做会幕门带卯的座和铜坛，并坛上的铜网和坛的一切器具，
chapters.en: And therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the brasen altar, and the brasen grate for it, and all the vessels of the altar,

chapters.zh: 并院子四围带卯的座和院门带卯的座，与帐幕一切的橛子和院子四围所有的橛子。
chapters.en: And the sockets of the court round about, and the sockets of the court gate, and all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins of the court round about.

chapters.zh: 比撒列用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线做精致的衣服，在圣所用以供职，又为亚伦做圣衣，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And of the blue, and purple, and scarlet, they made cloths of service, to do service in the holy , and made the holy garments for Aaron; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他用金线和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻做以弗得；
chapters.en: And he made the ephod gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.

chapters.zh: 把金子锤成薄片，剪出线来，与蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，用巧匠的手工一同绣上。
chapters.en: And they did beat the gold into thin plates, and cut wires, to work in the blue, and in the purple, and in the scarlet, and in the fine linen, cunning work.

chapters.zh: 又为以弗得做两条相连的肩带，接连在以弗得的两头。
chapters.en: They made shoulderpieces for it, to couple together: by the two edges was it coupled together.

chapters.zh: 其上巧工织的带子和以弗得一样的做法，用以束上，与以弗得接连一块，是用金线和蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻做的，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And the curious girdle of his ephod, that upon it, of the same, according to the work thereof; gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 又琢出两块红玛瑙，镶在金槽上，彷佛刻图书，按著以色列儿子的名字雕刻；
chapters.en: And they wrought onyx stones inclosed in ouches of gold, graven, as signets are graven, with the names of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 将这两块宝石安在以弗得的两条肩带上，为以色列人做纪念石，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And he put them on the shoulders of the ephod, stones for a memorial to the children of Israel; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他用巧匠的手工做胸牌，和以弗得一样的做法，用金线与蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻做的。
chapters.en: And he made the breastplate cunning work, like the work of the ephod; gold, blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.

chapters.zh: 胸牌是四方的，叠为两层；这两层长一虎口，宽一虎口，
chapters.en: It was foursquare; they made the breastplate double: a span the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof, doubled.

chapters.zh: 上面镶著宝石四行：第一行是红宝石、红璧玺、红玉；
chapters.en: And they set in it four rows of stones: row a sardius, a topaz, and a carbuncle: this the first row.

chapters.zh: 第二行是绿宝石、蓝宝石、金钢石；
chapters.en: And the second row, an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

chapters.zh: 第三行是紫玛瑙、白玛瑙、紫晶；
chapters.en: And the third row, a ligure, an agate, and an amethyst.

chapters.zh: 第四行是水苍玉、红玛瑙、碧玉。这都镶在金槽中。
chapters.en: And the fourth row, a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper: inclosed in ouches of gold in their inclosings.

chapters.zh: 这些宝石都是按著以色列十二个儿子的名字，彷佛刻图书，刻十二个支派的名字。
chapters.en: And the stones according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names, the engravings of a signet, every one with his name, according to the twelve tribes.

chapters.zh: 在胸牌上，用精金拧成如绳子的炼子。
chapters.en: And they made upon the breastplate chains at the ends, wreathen work pure gold.

chapters.zh: 又做两个金槽和两个金环，安在胸牌的两头。
chapters.en: And they made two ouches gold, and two gold rings; and put the two rings in the two ends of the breastplate.

chapters.zh: 把那两条拧成的金炼子穿过胸牌两头的环子，
chapters.en: And they put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings on the ends of the breastplate.

chapters.zh: 又把炼子的那两头接在两槽上，安在以弗得前面肩带上。
chapters.en: And the two ends of the two wreathen chains they fastened in the two ouches, and put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod, before it.

chapters.zh: 做两个金环，安在胸牌的两头，在以弗得里面的边上，
chapters.en: And they made two rings of gold, and put on the two ends of the breastplate, upon the border of it, which on the side of the ephod inward.

chapters.zh: 又做两个金环，安在以弗得前面两条肩带的下边，挨近相接之处，在以弗得巧工织的带子以上。
chapters.en: And they made two golden rings, and put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath, toward the forepart of it, over against the coupling thereof, above the curious girdle of the ephod.

chapters.zh: 用一条蓝细带子把胸牌的环子和以弗得的环子系住，使胸牌贴在以弗得巧工织的带子上，不可与以弗得离缝，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And they did bind the breastplate by his rings unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it might be above the curious girdle of the ephod, and that the breastplate might not be loosed from the ephod; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他用织工做以弗得的外袍，颜色全是蓝的。
chapters.en: And he made the robe of the ephod woven work, all blue.

chapters.zh: 袍上留一领口，口的周围织出领边来，彷佛铠甲的领口，免得破裂。
chapters.en: And an hole in the midst of the robe, as the hole of an habergeon, a band round about the hole, that it should not rend.

chapters.zh: 在袍子底边上，用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻做石榴，
chapters.en: And they made upon the hems of the robe pomegranates blue, and purple, and scarlet, twined .

chapters.zh: 又用精金做铃铛，把铃铛钉在袍子周围底边上的石榴中间：
chapters.en: And they made bells pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates upon the hem of the robe, round about between the pomegranates;

chapters.zh: 一个铃铛一个石榴，一个铃铛一个石榴，在袍子周围底边上用以供职，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: A bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, round about the hem of the robe to minister ; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他用织成的细麻布为亚伦和他的儿子做内袍，
chapters.en: And they made coats fine linen woven work for Aaron, and for his sons,

chapters.zh: 并用细麻布做冠冕和华美的裹头巾，用撚的细麻布做裤子，
chapters.en: And a mitre fine linen, and goodly bonnets fine linen, and linen breeches fine twined linen,

chapters.zh: 又用蓝色、紫色、朱红色线，并撚的细麻，以绣花的手工做腰带，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And a girdle fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, needlework; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 他用精金做圣冠上的牌，在上面按刻图书之法，刻著归耶和华为圣。
chapters.en: And they made the plate of the holy crown pure gold, and wrote upon it a writing, the engravings of a signet, HOLINESS TO THE LORD.

chapters.zh: 又用一条蓝细带子将牌系在冠冕上，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And they tied unto it a lace of blue, to fasten on high upon the mitre; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 帐幕，就是会幕，一切的工就这样做完了。凡耶和华所吩咐摩西的，以色列人都照样作了。
chapters.en: Thus was all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation finished: and the children of Israel did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so did they.

chapters.zh: 他们送到摩西那里。帐幕和帐幕的一切器具，就是钩子、板、闩、柱子、带卯的座，
chapters.en: And they brought the tabernacle unto Moses, the tent, and all his furniture, his taches, his boards, his bars, and his pillars, and his sockets,

chapters.zh: 染红公羊皮的盖、海狗皮的顶盖，和遮掩柜的幔子，
chapters.en: And the covering of rams' skins dyed red, and the covering of badgers' skins, and the vail of the covering,

chapters.zh: 法柜和柜的杠并施恩座，
chapters.en: The ark of the testimony, and the staves thereof, and the mercy seat,

chapters.zh: 桌子和桌子的一切器具并陈设饼，
chapters.en: The table, all the vessels thereof, and the shewbread,

chapters.zh: 精金的灯台和摆列的灯盏，与灯台的一切器具，并点灯的油，
chapters.en: The pure candlestick, the lamps thereof, the lamps to be set in order, and all the vessels thereof, and the oil for light,

chapters.zh: 金坛、膏油、馨香的香料、会幕的门帘，
chapters.en: And the golden altar, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the hanging for the tabernacle door,

chapters.zh: 铜坛和坛上的铜网，坛的杠并坛的一切器具，洗濯盆和盆座，
chapters.en: The brasen altar, and his grate of brass, his staves, and all his vessels, the laver and his foot,

chapters.zh: 院子的帷子和柱子，并带卯的座，院子的门帘、绳子、橛子，并帐幕和会幕中一切使用的器具，
chapters.en: The hangings of the court, his pillars, and his sockets, and the hanging for the court gate, his cords, and his pins, and all the vessels of the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of the congregation,

chapters.zh: 精工做的礼服，和祭司亚伦并他儿子在圣所用以供祭司职分的圣衣。
chapters.en: The cloths of service to do service in the holy , and the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and his sons' garments, to minister in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 这一切工作都是以色列人照耶和华所吩咐摩西做的。
chapters.en: According to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so the children of Israel made all the work.

chapters.zh: 耶和华怎样吩咐的，他们就怎样做了。摩西看见一切的工都做成了，就给他们祝福。
chapters.en: And Moses did look upon all the work, and, behold, they had done it as the LORD had commanded, even so had they done it: and Moses blessed them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 正月初一日，你要立起帐幕，
chapters.en: On the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 把法柜安放在里面，用幔子将柜遮掩。
chapters.en: And thou shalt put therein the ark of the testimony, and cover the ark with the vail.

chapters.zh: 把桌子搬进去，摆设上面的物。把灯台搬进去，点其上的灯。
chapters.en: And thou shalt bring in the table, and set in order the things that are to be set in order upon it; and thou shalt bring in the candlestick, and light the lamps thereof.

chapters.zh: 把烧香的金坛安在法柜前，挂上帐幕的门帘。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set the altar of gold for the incense before the ark of the testimony, and put the hanging of the door to the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 把燔祭坛安在帐幕门前。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 把洗濯盆安在会幕和坛的中间，在盆里盛水。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and shalt put water therein.

chapters.zh: 又在四围立院帷，把院子的门帘挂上。
chapters.en: And thou shalt set up the court round about, and hang up the hanging at the court gate.

chapters.zh: 用膏油把帐幕和其中所有的都抹上，使帐幕和一切器具成圣，就都成圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that therein, and shalt hallow it, and all the vessels thereof: and it shall be holy.

chapters.zh: 又要抹燔祭坛和一切器具，使坛成圣，就都成为至圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt anoint the altar of the burnt offering, and all his vessels, and sanctify the altar: and it shall be an altar most holy.

chapters.zh: 要抹洗濯盆和盆座，使盆成圣。
chapters.en: And thou shalt anoint the laver and his foot, and sanctify it.

chapters.zh: 要使亚伦和他儿子到会幕门口来，用水洗身。
chapters.en: And thou shalt bring Aaron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and wash them with water.

chapters.zh: 要给亚伦穿上圣衣，又膏他，使他成圣，可以给我供祭司的职分；
chapters.en: And thou shalt put upon Aaron the holy garments, and anoint him, and sanctify him; that he may minister unto me in the priest's office.

chapters.zh: 又要使他儿子来，给他们穿上内袍。
chapters.en: And thou shalt bring his sons, and clothe them with coats:

chapters.zh: 怎样膏他们的父亲，也要照样膏他们，使他们给我供祭司的职分。他们世世代代凡受膏的，就永远当祭司的职任。
chapters.en: And thou shalt anoint them, as thou didst anoint their father, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office: for their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations.

chapters.zh: 摩西这样行，都是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: Thus did Moses: according to all that the LORD commanded him, so did he.

chapters.zh: 第二年正月初一日，帐幕就立起来。
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the first month in the second year, on the first of the month, the tabernacle was reared up.

chapters.zh: 摩西立起帐幕，安上带卯的座，立上板，穿上闩，立起柱子。
chapters.en: And Moses reared up the tabernacle, and fastened his sockets, and set up the boards thereof, and put in the bars thereof, and reared up his pillars.

chapters.zh: 在帐幕以上搭罩棚，把罩棚的顶盖盖在其上，是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: And he spread abroad the tent over the tabernacle, and put the covering of the tent above upon it; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 又把法版放在柜里，把杠穿在柜的两旁，把施恩座安在柜上。
chapters.en: And he took and put the testimony into the ark, and set the staves on the ark, and put the mercy seat above upon the ark:

chapters.zh: 把柜抬进帐幕，挂上遮掩柜的幔子，把法柜遮掩了，是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: And he brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the vail of the covering, and covered the ark of the testimony; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 又把桌子安在会幕内，在帐幕北边，在幔子外。
chapters.en: And he put the table in the tent of the congregation, upon the side of the tabernacle northward, without the vail.

chapters.zh: 在桌子上将饼陈设在耶和华面前，是照耶和华所吩咐他的
chapters.en: And he set the bread in order upon it before the LORD; as the LORD had commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 又把灯台安在会幕内，在帐幕南边，与桌子相对，
chapters.en: And he put the candlestick in the tent of the congregation, over against the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward.

chapters.zh: 在耶和华面前点灯，是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: And he lighted the lamps before the LORD; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 把金坛安在会幕内的幔子前，
chapters.en: And he put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail:

chapters.zh: 在坛上烧了馨香料做的香，是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: And he burnt sweet incense thereon; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 又挂上帐幕的门帘。
chapters.en: And he set up the hanging the door of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 在会幕的帐幕门前，安设燔祭坛，把燔祭和素祭献在其上，是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: And he put the altar of burnt offering the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation, and offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 把洗濯盆安在会幕和坛的中间，盆中盛水，以便洗濯。
chapters.en: And he set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar, and put water there, to wash .

chapters.zh: 摩西和亚伦并亚伦的儿子在这盆里洗手洗脚。
chapters.en: And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat:

chapters.zh: 他们进会幕或就近坛的时候，便都洗濯，是照耶和华所吩咐他的。
chapters.en: When they went into the tent of the congregation, and when they came near unto the altar, they washed; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 在帐幕和坛的四围立了院帷，把院子的门帘挂上。这样，摩西就完了工。
chapters.en: And he reared up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the hanging of the court gate. So Moses finished the work.

chapters.zh: 当时，云彩遮盖会幕，耶和华的荣光就充满了帐幕。
chapters.en: Then a cloud covered the tent of the congregation, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 摩西不能进会幕；因为云彩停在其上，并且耶和华的荣光充满了帐幕。
chapters.en: And Moses was not able to enter into the tent of the congregation, because the cloud abode thereon, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 每逢云彩从帐幕收上去，以色列人就起程前往；
chapters.en: And when the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward in all their journeys:

chapters.zh: 云彩若不收上去，他们就不起程，直等到云彩收上去。
chapters.en: But if the cloud were not taken up, then they journeyed not till the day that it was taken up.

chapters.zh: 日间，耶和华的云彩是在帐幕以上；夜间，云中有火，在以色列全家的眼前。在他们所行的路上都是这样。
chapters.en: For the cloud of the LORD upon the tabernacle by day, and fire was on it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
